title: MUST-FIX backlog — objective, source-confirmed defects (구현 전 필수 수정)
method: TIER 3 re-adjudication (workflow w9gt6mb6z, 36 domains). Each CL-C(HIGH/both)+GP-A(confident) finding classified OBJECTIVE-defect vs subjective vs false-positive; objectives re-confirmed against source.

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.defect: V0 비용 상한이 §1.5에서는 ₩0(로컬)으로 정의되지만, §3.2 GO/NO-GO 항목 #3에서는 'V0 비용 상한 = V1 동일', 항목 #14에서는 '비용 엔진 ₩40,000/월 하드코딩'으로 기재되어 동일 버전의 비용 기준이 정면 충돌한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: V0 구현 시 Cost Gate 임계값을 ₩0으로 설정할지 ₩40,000으로 설정할지 결정 불가능 — 어느 쪽을 선택해도 나머지 문서를 위반한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: V0 비용 상한을 단일 LOCK으로 확정하라. §1.5와 §3.2 #3/#14 중 하나를 정본으로 지정하고, 나머지를 DEPRECATE 처리한 후 config.v1.toml에 반영하라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: §1.5: '비용 상한 | ₩0 (로컬)' vs §3.2 #3: 'V0 비용 상한 = V1 동일 명시' and #14: '비용 엔진 ₩40,000/월 하드코딩'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.defect: GOVERNANCE §1.2/§1.5는 V0=L1(수동), V1=L2(COPILOT)로 정의하나, 정본인 GLOSSARY_CROSS_DOMAIN §14는 L0=MANUAL, L1=SUPERVISED, L2=COPILOT로 정의하여 GOVERNANCE 기준이 정본 대비 1레벨 오프셋되어 있다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: GOVERNANCE가 'V0=L1'이라 할 때 구현체가 autonomy_level='L1'을 설정하면 정본 기준 SUPERVISED(감독 모드)가 되어 V0의 설계 의도인 MANUAL과 다르게 동작한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: GOVERNANCE §1.2/§1.5의 자율도 단계를 정본(GLOSSARY §14, 3-10 LOCK-AP)에 맞게 V0=L0(MANUAL), V1=L1(SUPERVISED)... 로 수정하거나, LOCK L1 값을 'L0'으로 수정하라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: GOVERNANCE L25: 'V0=L1(수동), V1=L2(COPILOT)' vs GLOSSARY L166: 'L0(MANUAL) → L1(SUPERVISED) → L2(COPILOT)'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.defect: §2.9 헤더는 '20개 도메인(1-1~6-13)이 R1~R11 전체 준수 대상'이라 선언하나 실제 테이블은 T5(5-4)까지만 20행을 나열하고 Tier 6(6-1~6-13) 13개 도메인 및 4-4 MLOps가 전부 누락되어 범위 선언이 허위다.

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: §2.9 테이블을 36개 도메인 전수로 재작성하거나, 헤더 범위 선언을 실제 테이블과 일치하도록 수정하라. R_RULE_COMPLIANCE_MATRIX와 정합성 확인 필수.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: GOVERNANCE L229: '20개 도메인(1-1 ~ 6-13)은 본 문서(0-0)의 global R1~R11 전체 준수 대상'; table L232-253 ends at '5-4 Error-Handling'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: GOVERNANCE §2.9의 3-10 명칭을 'Agent-Protocol'로 수정하라. 'Finance-Investing'이 별도 도메인(예: AI-Investing)과의 혼동에서 비롯된 경우 CONFLICT_LOG에 기록하라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: GOVERNANCE L246: '| T3 | 3-10 Finance-Investing |' vs DEPENDENCY_GRAPH §6.1: '3-10 Agent-Protocol | P1 LangGraph+MCP'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: ID가 없는 DEFINED-HERE 항목은 LOCK/FREEZE 변경 발생 시 영향 도메인 통지(R-T0-1)를 수행할 수 없어 LOCK 동결 계약이 사실상 미이행된다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: 4-1의 7건에 LOCK-RT-15~21 ID를 즉시 부여하고 레지스트리에 등재하라. 총계 40이 이미 이를 포함한 경우 변동 없음을, 미포함이면 47로 수정하라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: _index.md L61: '4-1에는 ... 7건의 미명명 DEFINED-HERE 항목이 추가로 존재. 향후 LOCK-RT-15~21 부여 예정.'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: 거버넌스 감사 기준으로 §7을 참조하면 의존성 그래프가 완성된 것으로 판단하나 실제로는 역참조 누락이 남아 구현 단계의 소비 도메인이 공급 도메인을 명시하지 않게 된다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: §5.2 상세 항목의 현재 실제 상태를 확인하여, 해결 완료된 항목은 ❌를 제거하고 미해결 항목은 §7 RESOLVED를 OPEN으로 되돌려 일치시켜라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: §5.2 L329: '1-1 Verifier | ✅ | ❌ | 역참조 추가 필요'; §7 L464: '횡단 역참조 누락 (6-2) | 9건 → ✅ 전체 RESOLVED'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.defect: R_RULE_COMPLIANCE_MATRIX 목적 헤더(L2)는 '34개 도메인 감사'라 하나, §4 통계(L132)는 '36개 도메인'을 기록하고, VERSION_ROADMAP_CONFLICTS(L19)는 '35개 도메인 교차 확인'이라 한다. 세 거버넌스 문서가 서로 다른 도메인 수를 기준으로 한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: 감사 범위가 34/35/36 중 어느 것인지 불명확하면 전수 감사 완료 여부를 객관적으로 판단할 수 없고 누락 도메인이 발생한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: 현재 정본 도메인 수를 36개로 확정하여 LOCK하고, '34개' 및 '35개' 기준 헤더는 superseded 처리하거나 갱신하라.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: R_RULE_COMPLIANCE_MATRIX L2: '34개 도메인 계획서의 글로벌 거버넌스 R1~R11 준수 현황 전수 감사'; §4 L132: '전체 도메인 수 | 36'

0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.defect: S8-5는 R-63-7 위임 깊이 임계값이 '6-3 문서: V1: 3단계'이나 LOCK-AT-004(V1 config=2)에 따르면 '2단계 초과 시 거부'가 정확하다고 인정하면서도 6-3 원본 도메인 문서의 수정 없이 'Phase 1 구현 전 수정 필요'로 이월했다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.why_objective: 구현자가 6-3 도메인 문서를 참조하면 위임 깊이를 3으로 구현하지만 실제 LOCK-AT-004 기준은 2이므로 런타임에서 유효한 위임을 잘못 거부하거나 허용하는 오프-바이-원 버그가 발생한다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.fix: 6-3 Agent-Teams 도메인 문서의 R-63-7 위임 깊이를 'V1: 2단계 (LOCK-AT-004, config=2)로 수정하라. S8-5의 이월 노트만으로는 원본 충돌이 해소되지 않는다.
0-0_Governance-Rules-Meta.evidence_quote: S8-5 L288: 'R-63-7 위임 깊이 임계값 off-by-one — "V1: 3단계"로 기술되어 있으나 LOCK-AT-004(V1 config=2, 상한=3)에 따르면 "V1: 2단계 초과 시 거부"가 정확. Phase 1 구현 전 수정 필요.'

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: Permission Level definitions in 상세명세 §1.1 (NONE/READ_ONLY/EXECUTE/WRITE/CROSS_DOMAIN/ADMIN, Level 0-5) directly contradict LOCK-BN-02 canonical values (Level 0=읽기전용/1=생성/2=수정/3=실행/4=외부통신/5=금융). Same level number carries different semantics across the spec corpus, making permission enforcement ambiguous—e.g., Level 2 means EXECUTE in 상세명세 but 수정(Modify) per LOCK.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: Two incompatible Permission Level schemas exist simultaneously in the spec; any implementation that follows 상세명세 §1.1 will assign wrong permissions vs LOCK-BN-02, violating the canonical LOCK. This is a correctness defect, not a preference.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Replace 상세명세 §1.1 table with the LOCK-BN-02 canonical table (읽기전용/생성/수정/실행/외부통신/금융), or mark 상세명세 §1 as superseded by 01_permission-matrix/_index.md which correctly implements LOCK-BN-02.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: | 1 | `READ_ONLY` | 읽기 전용 | 자기 도메인 데이터 읽기 | (상세명세 L29) vs LOCK-BN-02: Level 1 생성 (AUTHORITY_CHAIN.md L54)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: DynamicPermissionAdjuster in 상세명세 §1.3 applies `adjustments.append(+1)` when trust_score>0.9, enabling runtime privilege escalation (e.g., base_level=4 → effective=5=ADMIN). LOCK-BN-17 mandates 'Only Stricter' direction; the canonical 01_permission-matrix/_index.md §5.2 explicitly enforces downward-only adjustment (`min(0, total_adjustment)`). The 상세명세 code contradicts both.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: An upward permission adjustment bypasses the Gate/approval path required by LOCK-BN-08/10, granting ADMIN-level access autonomously based solely on a trust_score metric.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Remove `adjustments.append(+1)` from 상세명세 §1.3. Any upward permission change must route through 07 Gate approval. Align with 01_permission-matrix/_index.md RULES table (all deltas negative).
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote:
if context.trust_score > 0.9:
    adjustments.append(+1)  # 신뢰도 높으면 레벨 상향 (상세명세 L79)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: LOCK-BN-13 is quoted in §1 of v3_instances.md as: 'candidate_node_cap V1=3 … V3=100 (V1=5, V2=20, V3=100)'. The same row states V1=3 and V1=5 simultaneously. AUTHORITY_CHAIN.md and 04_node-lifecycle/_index.md both define LOCK-BN-13 V1=5 unambiguously.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: A LOCK verbatim-citation row self-contradicts (V1 appears as both 3 and 5). Any implementation reading this canonical catalog cannot determine the V1 candidate cap without consulting a different file, making the catalog unreliable.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Correct v3_instances.md §1 LOCK-BN-13 row to read: 'candidate_node_cap V1=5, V2=20, V3=100' (remove the erroneous leading 'V1=3'). Audit how 'V1=3' entered—it is the active_node_cap value (LOCK-BN-12), not candidate_node_cap.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: | LOCK-BN-13 | D2.0-03 §3.2.1(B) | candidate_node_cap V1=3 … V3=100 (V1=5, V2=20, V3=100) | (v3_instances.md L21)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: CandidateNodeCap in 상세명세 §4.3 sets max_candidates=20 and max_active=5 as defaults, contradicting LOCK-BN-12 (active V1=3) and LOCK-BN-13 (candidate V1=5). These values match no canonical version tier: max_candidates=20 is V2 candidate cap; max_active=5 is V1 candidate cap—the wrong LOCK applied to the wrong field.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: An implementer following 상세명세 §4.3 defaults would allow 5 active nodes (V2 level) and 20 candidates in V1, violating both active and candidate LOCKs for V1.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Replace 상세명세 §4.3 CandidateNodeCap defaults with LOCK-BN-12 (max_active=3 V1) and LOCK-BN-13 (max_candidates=5 V1), and make them version-parameterized per the canonical 04_node-lifecycle/_index.md §5.1 table.

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: LOCK-BN-07 requires 'session_end → 즉시 OFF'. T12 (ACTIVE→DRAINING on session_end) is marked '즉시' but T13 (BUSY→DRAINING on session_end) says '진행 태스크 완료 후'. This means a BUSY P2 node is not immediately terminated on session end, contradicting the LOCK. Additionally, 05_memory-sharing §7.3 explicitly preserves PRIVATE slots during T13 drain, compounding the LOCK-BN-07 violation.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: LOCK-BN-07 (D2.0-03 §3.3.2 Option A) is an absolute LOCK. T13's deferred termination violates it. An implementer following the transition table will produce behavior that contradicts the LOCK for any BUSY P2 node.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Either amend T13 to immediately cancel in-flight tasks on session_end (LOCK-BN-07 compliant), or formally supersede LOCK-BN-07 with a Decision record permitting graceful drain. The current state is a contradictory spec.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: T13 | BUSY | DRAINING | session_end | P2 세션 종료 (진행 태스크 완료 후) (04_node-lifecycle/_index.md L104) vs LOCK-BN-07: 세션 종료 → 즉시 OFF

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: MCP tier→Permission Level mapping table (§6.2) labels levels using 상세명세's wrong nomenclature: free=1 'READ_ONLY', basic=2 'EXECUTE', premium=3 'WRITE', enterprise=4 'CROSS_DOMAIN'. These labels contradict LOCK-BN-02 where Level 1=생성, 2=수정, 3=실행, 4=외부통신. An external client mapped to 'EXECUTE'(basic=2) actually gets 수정(Modify) rights per LOCK-BN-02.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: MCP external clients' effective permissions will be misapplied if implementers use the label descriptions rather than the numeric level against LOCK-BN-02's semantics, causing wrong access control decisions.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Replace tier table label column with LOCK-BN-02 canonical names (생성/수정/실행/외부통신) and add a note that the level numeric values are per LOCK-BN-02.

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: ActivationGateResult.all_passed() unconditionally requires approval_result == 'allow'. Guard G1 (§4.1) states 'P0는 면제' (P0 is exempt from 07 Approval). No special path bypasses all_passed() for P0 nodes. Any P0 activation call will fail because no approval is issued—approval_result will not be 'allow'—causing P0 auto-activation to break.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: P0 nodes are the foundational always-active nodes; broken P0 activation is a critical path correctness failure. The all_passed() contract is incompatible with P0 exemption.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Add domain_priority to ActivationGateResult and create an all_passed_p0() path (or not_required sentinel value) so P0 nodes can pass the gate without an approval token.

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: LifecycleFSM.TRANSITION_TABLE lists only 11 of 16 declared transitions. T08 (BUSY→SUSPENDED), T11 (BUSY→DRAINING on terminate_trigger), T13 (BUSY→DRAINING on session_end), T15 (SUSPENDED→TERMINATED), and T16 (ACTIVATING→SUSPENDED) are absent from the table. The code comment says '# ... 전체 16개 전이' but only 11 are defined.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: The FSM code is the implementation contract. Missing transitions mean BUSY→SUSPENDED suspend commands, BUSY drain on terminate, P2 session_end while BUSY, force-terminate from SUSPENDED, and activation failures will all raise StateInvalidError unhandled, breaking critical lifecycle paths.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Enumerate all 16 transitions (T01-T16) in TRANSITION_TABLE with their correct guard functions. Remove the placeholder comment.

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: LazyNodeGenerator.ensure_ready() performs LAZY→ACTIVATING→ACTIVE transitions but has no error handling. If connect_mcp(), allocate_memory(), or preload_templates() fails, the node is left in ACTIVATING state with no rollback path. T16 (ACTIVATING→SUSPENDED on activation_failed) exists in 04_lifecycle but the 상세명세 code doesn't implement it.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: A node stuck in ACTIVATING cannot receive tasks, be re-activated, or be terminated via normal paths, creating an irrecoverable state in the lifecycle FSM.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Wrap ensure_ready() in a try/except block; on any failure, emit activation_failed trigger to transition to SUSPENDED (T16) and clean up partial resources.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote:
async def ensure_ready(self, node_id: str) -> NodeHandle:
    if state == NodeState.LAZY:
        mcp_handle = await self.connect_mcp(node_id)
        memory = await self.allocate_memory(node_id)
        templates = await self.preload_templates(node_id)
        await self.transition(node_id, NodeState.ACTIVATING)
        await self.transition(node_id, NodeState.ACTIVE) (상세명세 L477-489)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: MCPBlueNodeServer._create_handler hardcodes auth_token="" (empty string) in the VamosMessage passed to node_handle.execute(). The comment states 'Auth Gateway에서 주입' but the handler closure creates and passes the message to execute() immediately—Auth Gateway token injection never occurs in the shown code path.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: All requests processed via this handler execute with no auth token, making every MCP-external call unauthenticated regardless of the caller's credentials.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Inject the auth token before constructing VamosMessage, or retrieve the resolved token from Auth Gateway before calling node_handle.execute(). Remove the empty-string placeholder from the message schema path.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: auth_token="",  # Auth Gateway에서 주입 (상세명세 L876) — message passed directly to node_handle.execute(request) at L882

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: The Node-to-Node block rule is specified as: detect violation when `source.node_type != 'core' AND target.node_type != 'core'`. This trusts self-declared node_type in the VamosMessage. A Blue Node that spoofs source.node_type='core' (or target.node_type='core') bypasses the LOCK-BN-14 block entirely—the condition becomes false and no violation is detected.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: LOCK-BN-14 prohibits direct N2N communication. The enforcement predicate relies on unverified message fields, creating an authz bypass on the architectural control.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Node_type verification must be performed against an authenticated registry, not the message's self-declared field. The Broker should resolve node_type from a trusted NodeRegistry keyed by authenticated node_id, not from the message content.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: Broker->>Broker: LOCK-BN-14 위반 탐지 (source.node_type != "core" AND target.node_type != "core") (integration_test_core_bn.md L214)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: MCPBridgeLayer.base_url validator accepts both http:// and https:// schemes. A comment notes 'dev/test 환경 호환을 위해 http 도 허용', but there is no environment flag or production-guard preventing http:// from being used in production. TLS 1.3 is mandated in the security section (§12.2) but is not enforced at the schema-validation layer.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: Production instances can be configured with http:// and pass schema validation, sending all MCP traffic unencrypted, violating the stated TLS 1.3 requirement.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Change the validator to only accept https:// by default; allow http:// only when an explicit allow_insecure_http=True flag is set AND environment is not 'production'.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: # 프로덕션은 https 권장 (TLS 1.3, §12.2). dev/test 환경 호환을 위해 http 도 허용. (07_mcp-bridge/_index.md L139-140)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: LOCK-BN-15 defines 'max 3 simultaneous active Nodes' (node-count constraint). Policy Overrides §3 applies this as max_concurrent_calls=3 'BN당 동시 호출 상한' (calls-per-node constraint), asserting these are equivalent. They are orthogonal resource axes: active node count ≠ concurrent calls per node.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: Applying a node-count LOCK as a per-node call limit means: (a) the actual 3-node cap is unprotected, and (b) each P0 node is limited to 3 concurrent calls, a restriction the LOCK never mandated.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Separate max_active_nodes (tied to LOCK-BN-15) from max_calls_per_node (a MODULE-ARCH decision). Remove the erroneous equivalence assertion in §3.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: | **resource** | `max_concurrent_calls` | 3 | max | ↓ | BN당 동시 호출 상한 (= LOCK-BN-15 동시 실행 Node 상한과 정합) | (06_policy-overrides/_index.md L190)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: GR_REQUIRE_CITATION guardrail is listed in 03's catalog with action=block and checks evidence_refs being empty. It is applied at the 'pre' injection phase (시스템 프롬프트+도메인 규칙 입력). However, evidence_refs is an output field of NodeResponseEnvelope—it does not exist at pre-injection time. The pre stage cannot evaluate post-stage output fields.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: Attempting to check evidence_refs at pre time will always see an empty value (the response hasn't been generated yet), causing all web-research requests to be blocked incorrectly, or requiring the guardrail to silently no-op, making its purpose moot.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Mark GR_REQUIRE_CITATION with applicable_phase=post and assign ownership to 06_policy-overrides (response validation), not 03_template-injection (pre-injection). Add phase discrimination to all guardrail catalog entries.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: | `GR_REQUIRE_CITATION` | rule_based | HIGH | block | evidence_refs 비어있으면 차단 | TS_WEB_RESEARCH | (03_template-injection/_index.md L433) applied at pre stage (L455-457)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: RESOLVER_TIMEOUT is declared as a failure code with recovery strategy 'global-only ResolvedPolicy fallback'. However, PolicyResolver.resolve() pseudocode contains no timeout parameter, no asyncio timeout wrapper, no fallback_policy generation path, and no RESOLVER_TIMEOUT emission. The documented recovery is entirely absent from the algorithm.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: When PolicyResolver hangs (e.g., policy_store unavailable), there is no timeout-triggered fallback. The 07 Gate receives no input, blocking all execution on the critical path—a correctness failure on a mandatory component.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Add resolver_timeout_ms to policy configuration. Wrap resolve() with asyncio.wait_for(), emit bn.policy.resolver_timeout on timeout, and return a global-only ResolvedPolicy as documented.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.evidence_quote: async def resolve(self, node_id: str, request_id: str) -> ResolvedPolicy: (06_policy-overrides/_index.md L352) — no timeout wrapper or fallback path present, vs error table: RESOLVER_TIMEOUT → fallback: global-only ResolvedPolicy (L599)

2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.defect: PolicyResolver.resolve() accepts new policy parameters (not in global baseline) from domain/node scopes and logs MISSING_GLOBAL_BASELINE as INFO, then adds them to ResolvedPolicy. These are then consumed by 07 Gate. A domain/node can introduce arbitrary policy keys that the Gate uses, effectively expanding policy scope without global approval—violating LOCK-BN-17's 'Only Stricter' principle (no base = no comparison).
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.why_objective: Unreviewed policy parameters added at domain/node level bypass the global policy registry, enabling domain-level extension of policy semantics without ORANGE CORE approval.
2-1_Blue-Node-Architecture.fix: Change MISSING_GLOBAL_BASELINE from INFO to REJECT. New policy keys must be registered in the global baseline via ORANGE CORE Policy Registry before any domain/node can use them.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: BrainRequest is instantiated with fields domain, complexity, and payload (e.g. domain='general', complexity='medium', payload={...}) that do not exist in the P1-1 canonical schema, which defines only task_type, prompt, system_prompt, tier, max_tokens, temperature, cost_cap, timeout_ms, tools, metadata, trace_id with Config.extra='forbid'. These instantiations would raise ValidationError at runtime.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: BrainRequest.Config.extra='forbid' means any unknown field causes a Pydantic ValidationError. The fields domain, complexity, payload are not in the schema definition at P1-1_brain_adapter_v1_spec.md lines 44-56.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Remove domain, complexity, payload from BrainRequest calls in e2e_reasoning_integration.md and encode intent via the existing fields (task_type, metadata, system_prompt). Alternatively, if these fields are needed, add them to the canonical BrainRequest schema and update Config.extra accordingly.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote:
class BrainRequest(BaseModel):
    task_type: Literal[...]
    prompt: str
    ...
    cost_cap: float = Field(..., ge=0.0)
    ...
    class Config: extra = "forbid"

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: ParallelExecutor.submit() pseudocode only does 'async with self.semaphore' then _execute — it never calls pending_queue.put(), never emits brain.parallel.queued event, and never measures queue_wait_ms. The fields pending_queue and active_tasks are declared but unused. PAR-2 scenario's expected queued event (brain.parallel.queued) and user_alert_sent=true are not realizable from this implementation.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: The spec simultaneously defines pending_queue as a queue object and the PAR-2 test scenario expecting brain.parallel.queued event with user_alert_sent=true, but the pseudocode implementation cannot produce this event because it only blocks on the semaphore without enqueuing.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Either (a) add explicit pending_queue.put() logic before semaphore acquisition and emit brain.parallel.queued on enqueue, or (b) remove the pending_queue declaration and the PAR-2 queued-event requirement and acknowledge that semaphore blocking is the sole queuing mechanism.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: async def submit(self, request: BrainRequest) -> ConnectorResponse:\n        async with self.semaphore:  # 상한 초과 시 자동 대기 (큐잉)\n            slot_id = self._acquire_slot()

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: select() is declared as a synchronous abstract method (`def select(...)`) in the BaseLLMRouter ABC, but §4.6 specifies that the router maintains an internal `asyncio.Semaphore(3)` for slot management. A synchronous method cannot await a semaphore, making the concurrent slot enforcement impossible to implement correctly within the declared contract.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Awaiting an asyncio.Semaphore requires an async context. A `def select()` synchronous method cannot await anything; calling `semaphore.acquire()` synchronously would block the event loop. The contract as written makes LOCK-69-2 parallel limit unimplementable.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Change the ABC signature to `async def select(...)` and update all callers. Or redesign slot acquisition to use a separate async lease/context-manager pattern that callers must await.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: @abstractmethod\n    def select(self, input: RoutingInput) -> tuple["BaseBrainAdapter", RoutingDecision]:\n        """최종 라우팅. 병렬 상한 위반 시 자동 큐잉(LOCK-69-2)."""

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: Router state S6 acquires a parallel slot and returns (adapter, decision) to the caller, who then calls invoke() and separately releases the slot. If the caller raises an exception, is cancelled, or the process dies after receiving the adapter but before releasing the slot, the slot is permanently leaked. With max 3 slots, 3 leaked slots cause deadlock for all subsequent requests.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: The spec explicitly states 'S6의 슬롯 해제는 비동기 (어댑터 invoke 완료). S1로의 전이는 라우팅 결정 완료 시점 기준이며 슬롯 점유는 별도 추적' — confirming the slot is held across a boundary the router does not control. This is a provable deadlock scenario.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Router must own the full invoke() lifecycle within a try/finally that releases the slot, or return a context-manager-based lease object that guarantees release on __exit__ even on exception/cancellation.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote:
R->>Slot: acquire slot (LOCK-69-2)
        ...
        R-->>C: (adapter, decision)
        C->>A: invoke(BrainRequest)
        A-->>C: ConnectorResponse
        C->>Slot: release

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: parallel_executor.md uses BrainRequest with domain and complexity fields (lines 97-106) that are not in the canonical BrainRequest schema. Also uses complexity='instant' which is not in any schema vocabulary. With Config.extra='forbid' these would raise ValidationError.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Same schema constraint as T8-001 — BrainRequest.Config.extra='forbid' rejects unknown fields. 'instant' is not in the BrainRequest Literal definition.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Remove domain and complexity from BrainRequest constructor calls in parallel_executor.md; encode domain/complexity intent via task_type and metadata fields that exist in the schema.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: BrainRequest.cost_cap is Field(..., ge=0.0) — a required field with no default. §5 defines global.cost_cap_default=1.0 but does not wire it as the Pydantic field default. Every caller must explicitly pass cost_cap or receive a ValidationError, which breaks internal callers (health-check routing, PAR-2 scenario requests) that omit it.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: The e2e_reasoning_integration.md and parallel_executor.md example BrainRequest calls omit cost_cap, which with Field(...) would raise ValidationError. The stated global default is unreachable via the schema as defined.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Change cost_cap field to `cost_cap: float = Field(1.0, ge=0.0)` using the global default, or inject the default at the adapter layer before constructing BrainRequest.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: cost_cap: float = Field(..., ge=0.0)           # LOCK-69-7: 비용 상한 (상대 단위)

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: Benchmark code at line 129 instantiates RoutingWeightUpdate with fields drift_metric='psi', drift_value=0.18, window='1h' — fields that do not exist in the canonical schema (drift_routing_integration.md §2.1). The schema has Config.extra='forbid' and the required fields drift_window_start, drift_window_end, drift_severity are omitted.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: RoutingWeightUpdate.Config.extra='forbid' means non-existent fields cause ValidationError. The canonical schema defines drift_window_start: datetime (required), drift_window_end: datetime (required), drift_severity: Literal (required) — none are supplied in the benchmark call.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Replace drift_metric, drift_value, window with the canonical fields: drift_window_start, drift_window_end (datetime values), drift_severity ('info'/'warn'/'critical'), and remove non-existent fields.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: resolve_config() uses `if key in self._config` to look up keys like 'ollama.host', but _load_yaml() returns a nested dict where the actual structure is hal.ollama.host. A flat string key 'ollama.host' will never be found in a nested dict, causing config.yaml to be silently ignored and always falling back to code defaults — breaking LOCK-69-6 priority.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Python dict lookup of 'ollama.host' in {'hal': {'ollama': {'host': '...'}}} returns KeyError/False, so the config.yaml layer is permanently bypassed. This is provable by inspection of the YAML structure vs the dict key used.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Either flatten the YAML on load (convert hal.ollama.host to key 'ollama.host'), or implement a path-aware lookup that navigates nested keys via dot notation.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: if key in self._config:\n            return HalConfigSource(key=key, value=str(self._config[key]), source="config.yaml")

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: P1-1 §11 cross-check table records that P1-1 expects `hal_v1.get_endpoint(provider: str) -> str`, but P1-2 defines get_endpoint() -> HalEndpoint (an object). A string and a HalEndpoint object are not substitutable — P1-1 callers expecting a string URL will get an object and break.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Return type str vs object HalEndpoint is a concrete type contract mismatch that is provably incompatible. The P1-1 cross-check table explicitly records the expected str return type while P1-2 source returns HalEndpoint.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Align the contract: either P1-2 get_endpoint() returns str (base_url directly) and P1-1 expectation is satisfied, or add a P1-1 note that callers must use .base_url attribute and update all call sites.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: P1-2 HAL V1 | BrainAdapter → HAL 호출 방향 | `BaseBrainAdapter.invoke` 가 HAL.get_endpoint(provider) 로 엔드포인트 결정 | P1-2 `hal_v1.get_endpoint(provider: str) -> str` 시그니처 요청

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: _index.md §5 defines brain.route.selected as a flat payload-centric JSON structure (timestamp, level, module, event, trace_id, payload{...}). P1-3 §8.1 defines the same event with a mandatory 4-block structure: error{}/context{}/recovery{}/trace_id at top level. These are incompatible schemas for the same event name.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: LOCK-69-10 downstream log validators and parsers cannot know which schema is canonical. Implementors will produce logs that fail one of the two specs. This is a provable schema contradiction for a mandated event.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Designate P1-3 §8.1 as the canonical schema (it implements R-01-7 R-69-2) and update _index.md §5 to match it exactly. Delete the flat-structure example from _index.md.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: Step 4 of invoke() pseudocode uses await inside a list comprehension: `[await ToolRegistry.invoke(tc.tool_id, ...) for tc in raw.tool_calls]`. This is invalid Python async syntax — await cannot be used inside a regular list comprehension; asyncio.gather is required for concurrent execution.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: In Python, `await` inside a list comprehension is a SyntaxError in async context. Tool calls execute serially and any SyntaxError would break the function entirely.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Replace with `await asyncio.gather(*[ToolRegistry.invoke(tc.tool_id, tc.arguments, trace_id=request.trace_id) for tc in raw.tool_calls])` for concurrent execution, or use a for-loop with sequential awaits if serial execution is intended.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote:
tool_calls_result = [
            await ToolRegistry.invoke(tc.tool_id, tc.arguments, trace_id=request.trace_id)
            for tc in raw.tool_calls
        ]

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: RoutingWeightUpdate schema accepted from external domain 4-4 carries no authentication, authorization, or integrity verification field — only trace_id. A spoofed or replayed RoutingWeightUpdate can downweight or upweight models, bypassing LOCK-69-7 cost controls by manipulating routing preferences.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: The update mutates live routing weights that govern which LLM is called and at what cost tier. No source authentication or signature means any process that can post to the apply_weight_update endpoint can alter routing behavior.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Add a required `source_auth` or HMAC signature field to RoutingWeightUpdate. Validate the signature against a shared secret between 4-4 and 6-9 before applying weight changes. Add replay protection via timestamp+nonce.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote:
class RoutingWeightUpdate(BaseModel):
    model_id: str
    quality_delta: Optional[float] = None
    ...
    trace_id: str                              # 4-4 측 추적 ID
    class Config:
        extra = "forbid"

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: The ENV variable VAMOS_ROUTING_MAX_CONCURRENT overrides routing.parallel.max_concurrent directly, allowing V1/V2 fixed limit of 3 and V3 Approval Gate to be bypassed by simply setting this env var. LOCK-69-2 mandates an approval workflow for V3 limit increases, but this ENV override has no such constraint.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: LOCK-69-2 explicitly requires 'V3=N 상향 시 승인 게이트' (approval gate for any increase beyond 3). A plain ENV override that works in all V-Phases directly contradicts this LOCK.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Remove VAMOS_ROUTING_MAX_CONCURRENT from the ENV override table for V1/V2. For V3, gate the ENV override: only accept it if an Approval Gate record exists and is current. Otherwise ignore and log a security warning.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: LiteLLM proxy is exposed on host port 4000:4000 with API keys for Anthropic, OpenAI, and DeepSeek injected into the container environment. The deployment spec defines no authentication, ACL, or network restriction for this port. Any process or user with network access to the host can make unlimited LLM API calls charged to production keys.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Unauthenticated access to an LLM proxy holding production API keys enables cost abuse and unauthorized model invocation, bypassing LOCK-69-7 cost controls at the proxy level before they reach the HAL layer.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Bind LiteLLM to Docker internal network only (remove host port mapping). If external access is needed, add LiteLLM master_key authentication, restrict via reverse proxy with allowlist, and add rate limiting.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: Redis is deployed with only maxmemory and eviction policy settings — no requirepass, no ACL, no TLS, no AOF/RDB persistence. Redis serves as the cost accumulation counter for LOCK-69-7 automatic blocking. With allkeys-lru eviction, cost counter data can be silently evicted, making the cost block unreliable. Without auth, any container can manipulate cost counters.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: If the cost counter is evicted or tampered with, LOCK-69-7 'CostBudget >= 100% auto-block' cannot trigger correctly, allowing unbounded spending. This is a DATA_LOSS defect for a security-critical counter.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Add requirepass/ACL to Redis. Persist cost counters via AOF or use a separate durable store (Postgres). Do not rely on allkeys-lru for cost-critical keys — use a dedicated Redis keyspace or key prefix with noeviction policy.

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: RoutingWeightUpdate schema has both quality_delta and new_score as Optional[float] = None with no mutual-exclusion validator and no defined precedence. Both can be set simultaneously, creating ambiguous semantics: the spec does not state whether quality_delta is applied to the current weight or new_score replaces it when both are non-None.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: Without a validator or spec-defined precedence rule, implementations will diverge. The DRIFT-1 scenario sets quality_delta=-0.30 with new_score=None; the benchmark sets both non-None. Any implementation that handles this differently breaks one of the two scenarios.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Add a Pydantic validator enforcing mutual exclusion: exactly one of quality_delta or new_score must be non-None. Or define explicit precedence (e.g., new_score takes priority when both set).
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: quality_delta: Optional[float] = None      # 품질 점수 변화 (-1.0 ~ +1.0)\n    ...\n    new_score: Optional[float] = None          # 신규 가중 점수 (정규화 후)

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: The Big-O note at §3.3 states the V1 worst-case total call count is '1 + 2 + 2 = 5', but the V1 timeout retry constant is v1_timeout_retries=3 (not 2), and U-FB-10 test case expects same_model_timeouts=3 recorded. Actual V1 worst case is 1 (primary) + 3 (same-model retries) + 2 (transitions) = 6 calls.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: The Big-O analysis is arithmetically wrong given the documented constant value 3. This matters for timeout budget planning: 3×30s=90s for retries alone vs the stated 2×30s=60s. Implementors will underestimate worst-case latency.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Correct §3.3 Big-O note to '1 + 3 + 2 = 6 (V1 한정)' to match v1_timeout_retries=3 and U-FB-10 expectation.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: Big-O**: 최악 `O(3)` 호출 (Phase 1 + Phase 2b 최대 2회) + Phase 3 V1 에서 동일 모델 최대 2회 추가 재시도. 총 호출 수 상한은 `1 + 2 + 2 = 5` (V1 한정)

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: percentile() at line 294 uses `s[int(len(s) * p / 100)]` with no upper-bound clamp. For p=100 and any non-empty list, int(len(s) * 100/100) == len(s), which is an out-of-bounds index causing IndexError.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: This is a provable off-by-one: for p=100 and len(s)=N, the formula yields index N which is >= len(s), raising IndexError. The function is called for p95 and p99 in report().
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Clamp the index: `idx = min(int(len(s) * p / 100), len(s) - 1)` or use `math.ceil(len(s) * p / 100) - 1` for nearest-rank percentile.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: def percentile(self, p: float) -> float:\n        s = sorted(self.latencies_ms)\n        return s[int(len(s) * p / 100)] if s else 0.0

6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.defect: LOCK-69-2 V3 parallel upper-bound is documented as 'V3=N' requiring approval workflow, but no maximum value for N is defined anywhere. VAMOS_PARALLEL_MAX env var defaults to 3 but has no upper cap. An approved N could conflict with external API rate limits (10 requests/min normal, 3/min high-cost per §6).
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.why_objective: An unbounded N that is merely 'approval gated' makes the approval gate meaningless without a specified cap. Concurrent requests exceeding API rate limits will fail, violating reliability contracts.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.fix: Define an explicit maximum cap for N (e.g., N <= 10 or N <= external rate limit / average request duration). Add this cap to the approval workflow criteria and the LOCK-69-2 definition.
6-9_Brain-Adapter-HAL.evidence_quote: LOCK-69-2 V3 병렬 상한 상향 승인 워크플로 — V1/V2=3 → V3=N 상향 시 승인 게이트 (P2 도메인 인간 승인 필수)

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: For explain_type='global', variable `instance` is never assigned, yet line 182 calls `model.predict(instance)` unconditionally, causing NameError at runtime. Additionally `base_value` and `plot` are only assigned on the SHAP-local or SHAP-global path respectively; the return statement at L175-184 references both unconditionally.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable reference is a correctness defect—certain explain_type/method combinations produce NameError, not degraded output.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Initialize `instance=None`, `base_value=None`, `plot=None` before the conditional branches, and guard each use. Skip `model.predict(instance)` when `instance is None`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: RETURN ShapLimeResponse(..., prediction=model.predict(instance), ...)  # line 182, `instance` only set inside explain_type=='local' branches

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Neo4j password 'vamos_kg_2024' is hardcoded as a default value in Cond018Config at line 800.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Hardcoded credential in a design spec is a direct secret exposure defect — any implementation using the default will ship with a known password.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace default with a SecretRef/environment variable reference (e.g., `neo4j_password: SecretRef = Field(default_factory=lambda: SecretRef('VAMOS_NEO4J_PASSWORD'))`). Never embed literal credentials.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: neo4j_password: str = "vamos_kg_2024"  # line 800

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: E3 index operations reference `op.index` (lines 144-147: `index_registry.create(op.index, ...)`, `bulk_indexer.upsert(op.index, ...)`), but IndexOp schema (E1) has no `index` field — only op/documents/document_id/schema_def. The index name cannot reach the operation.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Schema/code mismatch: a field used in the critical path does not exist in the defined model, making the index operation unimplementable as specced.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `index: str` field to IndexOp schema, or use the parent SearchRequest.index field and document which takes precedence.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: CASE "create":  index_registry.create(op.index, op.schema_def)  # line 144, op.index absent from IndexOp schema

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: LOCK cites DEC-014 QoD formula (relevance*0.30+accuracy*0.25+freshness*0.25+completeness*0.20) as authoritative, but this 4-factor formula is explicitly marked SUPERSEDED in D2.0-06.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Using a superseded formula as a LOCK anchor makes the implementation specification internally self-contradictory — implementors cannot determine which formula is correct.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Update the LOCK citation to reference the current (non-superseded) QoD formula from D2.0-06, and update the QoDEvaluator.score call arguments accordingly.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: LOCK cite 'D2.0-06 DEC-014: QoD formula relevance*0.30+accuracy*0.25+freshness*0.25+completeness*0.20' (4-factor) used as authoritative. This DEC-014 4-factor formula is explicitly marked SUPERSEDED.

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: The update path (line ~406-427) soft-deletes existing graph elements then re-ingests; if re-ingestion fails it warns and CONTINUEs — leaving elements in soft-deleted state permanently with no rollback or staged-commit pattern.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Knowledge is destroyed without rollback on re-ingest failure — provable data loss on a mutation path.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Use a shadow/staging namespace: ingest new elements first, then swap atomically. On failure, keep the original elements and discard the staging data.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: 재처리 실패 시 warn 후 CONTINUE한다. 기존 요소는 soft-deleted 상태로 남고 신규 요소는 생성되지 않는다

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `total_samples` is referenced at L273 (`traffic_actual_pct=len(raw_data)/total_samples*100`) but is never defined in the execute() scope — NameError on every call.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable makes the traffic accounting branch unconditionally fail.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Compute `total_samples = sum(len(ExperimentStore.get_metric(...)) for v in config.variants)` before the loop, or compute it from variant sample sizes accumulated in the loop.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: traffic_actual_pct=len(raw_data) / total_samples * 100  # line 273, total_samples not defined anywhere in scope

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `bayesian_ab_test(control_data, treatment_data, ...)` receives plain numeric lists, but the function body references `control.variant_id`, `treatment.variant_id`, and `primary_metric.metric_name` (L330-334) — all of which are out of scope (AttributeError).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Scope/attribute errors make the Bayesian comparison path unconditionally fail at runtime.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Pass `variant_id` and `metric_name` as explicit arguments to `bayesian_ab_test`, or reference them from the outer loop variables `control` and `primary_metric`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: RETURN PairwiseComparison(baseline=control.variant_id, challenger=treatment.variant_id, metric_name=primary_metric.metric_name, ...)  # line 330-334, control is a list here

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: TaskEventStore.query + empty-check fires INSUFFICIENT_DATA before the UserStore.exists check. A non-existent user has no events, so INSUFFICIENT_DATA is always returned instead of USER_NOT_FOUND, contradicting E7 test scenario 3.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Wrong error code returned for a defined test case — the spec explicitly expects USER_NOT_FOUND for a non-existent user, but the algorithm returns INSUFFICIENT_DATA.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Move `UserStore.exists(request.user_id)` check to before the `user_events IS empty` check.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: IF user_events IS empty: RETURN Err(VamosError(COND_015_INSUFFICIENT_DATA))  # line 213-214, precedes UserStore.exists check at line 216

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Dead/contradictory branch: line 312 sets `status='expired'` then immediately checks `IF status != 'expired': stale_count += 1` — the condition is always False, so stale_count is never incremented when a source becomes unavailable.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: The condition is provably always False after the preceding assignment — stale count will always be underreported for source-unavailable items.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Save the previous status before overwriting (`prev_status = status`), then check `IF prev_status != 'expired'`; or introduce a separate `expired_count` variable.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote:
status = "expired"  # line 312
IF status != "expired":  # line 313 — always False

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `seen_pairs` is referenced (line 506: `IF pair NOT IN seen_pairs`) and mutated (`seen_pairs.add(pair)`) inside `generate_candidate_pairs_full` but is never initialized, causing NameError when the O(n^2) > max_pairs path executes.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable causes unconditional runtime failure on the full-scan code path.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `seen_pairs = set()` immediately after `pairs = []` before the loop.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: IF pair NOT IN seen_pairs:  # line 506, seen_pairs never initialized in this function

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `backlink_count` is only initialized inside the `IF request.resolve_backlinks` block (line 266), but is unconditionally referenced in the stats computation at line 294. When `preserve_links=False` or `resolve_backlinks=False`, backlink_count is undefined — NameError.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable on a reachable code path.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Initialize `backlink_count = 0` (and `skipped = 0`) before the conditional block, not inside it.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: backlink_count = 0 / link_map = {}  # line 266, inside IF resolve_backlinks block only; referenced unconditionally at line 294

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `dep_weight = len(dependencies)*0.1` is unbounded, and `prefetch_priority = time_urgency*0.6 + dep_weight*0.2 + access_pattern.score*0.2`. With 11+ dependencies the dep_weight term exceeds 1.0 and the declared output field prefetch_priority (ge=0.0, le=1.0) is violated.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Formula produces values outside the declared schema constraint, a provable numeric correctness defect.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Cap dep_weight: `dep_weight = min(1.0, len(dependencies)*0.1)` before using in the priority formula.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: prefetch_priority = time_urgency*0.6 + dep_weight*0.2 + access_pattern.score*0.2  # line 237, dep_weight = len(dependencies)*0.1 at line 236, unbounded

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `cool_down` is only assigned inside the `IF score < open_threshold` branch (line 110), but line 136 returns `cool_down_seconds=cool_down` unconditionally. On the half_open/closed/throttle transition paths, `cool_down` is undefined — NameError.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable on all non-open-circuit paths — guaranteed runtime failure for the majority of calls.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Initialize `cool_down = 0` (or `None`) before the conditional branches.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote:
cool_down = config.cool_down_seconds  # line 110, inside IF score < config.open_threshold only
RETURN Ok(BackpressureResponse(..., cool_down_seconds=cool_down))  # line 136, unconditional

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Retry counter `attempts=+1` is unary plus (assigns the integer literal 1), not increment. Every retry resets attempts to 1, so `job.attempts < job.max_retries` never advances — jobs are retried infinitely and never reach the dead/DLQ state.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: This is a provable operator precedence bug; `=+1` is not `+=1` in Python/pseudocode context. The retry counter never advances.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Change `attempts=+1` to `attempts=job.attempts+1` (and persist as `job_store.update(job_id, state='queued', attempts=job.attempts+1)`).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: job_store.update(job_id, state="queued", attempts=+1)  # line 149, unary plus assigns 1 not increments

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: State assignment `state='queued' IF 'send' ELSE 'scheduled'` (line 116) evaluates the condition as a non-empty string literal `'send'` which is always truthy — schedule operations are always marked `queued` instead of `scheduled`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A non-empty string is always truthy in Python; the ELSE branch is dead code. This is a provable logic inversion.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Change to `state='queued' IF request.operation == 'send' ELSE 'scheduled'`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: state="queued" IF "send" ELSE "scheduled"  # line 116, string literal 'send' always truthy

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Both `subscribe` and `unsubscribe` cases call `subscription_store.upsert(request.subscription)` (line 130). Unsubscribe should delete or deactivate the subscription, not re-upsert it — the unsubscribe operation has no effect.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Unsubscribe doing the same operation as subscribe is a provable logic defect — a user cannot opt out.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Change unsubscribe to `subscription_store.delete(request.subscription.subscription_id)` or `subscription_store.update(id, disabled=True)`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: CASE "subscribe" | "unsubscribe":  subscription_store.upsert(request.subscription)  # line 129-130, unsubscribe never deletes

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: failover CASE references `current_primary` (L147) which is never derived/fetched in the pseudocode. Also `target_region` is referenced bare at L141 while everywhere else it is `request.target_region` — two undefined symbols in the critical failover path.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variables make the failover execution path unconditionally fail at runtime.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `current_primary = topology.get_primary()` and `target_region = request.target_region` at the start of the failover CASE, with null/health validation.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: IF target_region NOT IN cfg.regions  # line 141 (bare), current_primary used at line 147 — neither initialized in this scope

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: circuit_breaker is referenced in the route case (`circuit_breaker.is_open(...)` at L171) and toggle case (`circuit_breaker.set(...)` at L204), but only `route_table` and `rate_limiter` are loaded in execute() at L141-142 — `circuit_breaker` is undefined.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable on the route critical path causes NameError for every route call that reaches the circuit breaker check.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `circuit_breaker = CircuitBreaker.connect()` in the initialization block alongside route_table and rate_limiter.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote:
route_table = RouteTable.load()  # line 142
rate_limiter = RateLimiter.connect()  # line 143
# circuit_breaker never loaded
IF circuit_breaker.is_open(match.target_service):  # line 172

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Timeout check (step 5) is placed AFTER the operation dispatch (step 4) has already completed. A slow or hung operation can never be interrupted; the timeout check only reports elapsed time after the fact. This structural pattern is present in all 5 e-series API gateway files (037-041).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A post-execution timeout check cannot bound execution time — this is a design-level correctness defect, not a preference.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Wrap the dispatch call in an async deadline context (e.g., `asyncio.wait_for(dispatch(...), timeout=timeout_ms/1000)`) so the operation is actually cancelled on timeout.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: MATCH request.operation: ... # step 4: full dispatch  / IF elapsed_ms() > timeout_ms: RETURN Error(COND_037_TIMEOUT)  # step 5 after dispatch

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Timeout check (step 5) executes AFTER the synchronous operation dispatch (step 4) has already completed — timeout cannot cancel or bound a slow/hung connection pool operation. Same structural defect as the API gateway e-series.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Post-execution timeout check cannot preempt a blocking operation — provable correctness defect in timeout enforcement.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Wrap dispatch in an async context deadline (asyncio.wait_for or equivalent) before executing.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: 타임아웃 검사(step5)가 연산 실행(step4) 완료 '후' elapsed_ms 비교만 수행. 실제 취소(cancellation) 없이 초과만 사후 감지

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Timeout is checked post-hoc (step 5, after handler returns at step 4) rather than as a deadline. Additionally, switch/rollback are accepted without a prior deploy having occurred — no state-machine precondition guards for the deployment workflow.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Post-hoc timeout is proven non-functional for aborting hung deploys. Accepting rollback before deploy is a workflow state-machine violation.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Wrap handlers in async deadlines. Add state-machine precondition checks: verify a deploy exists before accepting switch/rollback.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: Timeout is checked post-hoc (step 5, after the handler returns at step 4) rather than enforced as a deadline

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: SPD and EOD use `(1 - request.threshold)` as the allowed tolerance (e.g. threshold=0.8 → tolerance=0.2). But E2 output example shows SPD threshold=0.1, value=-0.18 → passed=False (0.18 > 0.1). The pseudocode with threshold=0.8 would compute 0.18 ≤ 0.2 → passed=True, contradicting the example.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: The algorithm and the output example use different threshold semantics — at least one is wrong, making the bias threshold logic ambiguous and unimplementable as documented.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Align the algorithm and example: either use a dedicated SPD/EOD threshold field (e.g., `spd_threshold=0.1`) separate from the DI ratio threshold, or fix the output example to match the formula.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: E2 출력 예시(L158-159): statistical_parity threshold=0.1, value=-0.18→passed=False; algorithm uses abs(value) <= (1 - request.threshold=0.8) = 0.2 → would be passed=True

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `adapter` is assigned only inside `IF lora_adapter_id is not None` (step 2), but `build_metadata(request, backend, adapter)` at step 7 (line 191) is called unconditionally — NameError on any non-LoRA request.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable on the majority code path (non-LoRA is the common case).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Initialize `adapter = None` before the conditional block so it is always defined when passed to build_metadata.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: model_metadata=build_metadata(request, backend, adapter)  # line 191, adapter only assigned inside IF lora_adapter_id is not None block

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Error path uses `source == last_source` to detect all-sources-failed, but `last_source` is never defined/bound before the loop — NameError. Additionally, the condition only fires when the last-iterated source fails, missing cases where an earlier source is the only one available.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable makes the all-sources-failed error path unreachable (NameError fires instead of the correct VamosError).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace the condition with a post-loop check: `IF len(collected_data) == 0: RETURN Err(VamosError('COND_103_DATA_SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE', ...))`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: IF len(collected_data) == 0 AND source == last_source:  # line 163, last_source never defined

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: StyleReference.model_post_init raises ValueError (line 49) — a Python exception throw — directly violating LOCK-CD-05 (예외 throw 금지). The corresponding failure code COND_080_STYLE_REF_INVALID is never returned as a VamosError.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Violation of a declared LOCK (no-throw contract) is a contract defect; the exception bypasses the Result<T,VamosError> error handling chain.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace `raise ValueError(...)` with a Pydantic validator that returns a validation error, and ensure the calling code converts validation failures to `Err(VamosError('COND_080_STYLE_REF_INVALID', ...))`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: def model_post_init(self, __context):  if not any([self.style_image, self.style_name, self.text_prompt]):  raise ValueError(...)  # line 47-49

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: VWS total=sum([sleep, fitness, mood, social, productivity_balance]) at line 292 sums the INPUT source objects (AggregatedSleep etc.) rather than the computed *_to_20 numeric sub-scores — produces TypeError or a meaninglessly large/wrong total.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Summing domain objects instead of numeric scores is a provable type error that produces wrong output on every call.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace with `total=sum([sleep_score, fitness_score, mood_score, social_score, productivity_score])` where each variable holds the already-computed float from the respective `*_to_20()` call.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: total=sum([sleep, fitness, mood, social, productivity_balance])  # line 292, these are AggregatedSleep/etc. objects not the computed *_to_20 floats

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: VWS computation calls sleep_to_20(sleep.*), fitness_to_20(fitness.*), etc. unconditionally, but the input validator requires only >=2 of 5 sources. Any absent source (e.g., sleep=None) causes AttributeError on .avg_quality_score.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: The validator explicitly allows absent sources, yet the computation accesses attributes on them unconditionally — guaranteed AttributeError on valid inputs.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Guard each *_to_20 call: `sleep_score = sleep_to_20(...) if sleep is not None else 0.0`. Apply similarly to all five axes.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: sleep=sleep_to_20(sleep.avg_quality_score, ...)  # line 287, sleep may be None when >=2 other sources provided

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: handle_trigger polls using `since=sub.subscription_expires_at or last_poll_cursor` — using subscription expiry time as the poll cursor is semantically wrong. If expiry is in the future, only future events are fetched. If `last_poll_cursor` is also undefined, this causes NameError.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Using expiry timestamp as poll cursor is a logic error that causes event loss (future-only events) or cursor drift under normal operation.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace `sub.subscription_expires_at` with a dedicated `sub.last_polled_at` cursor field and persist it after each successful poll.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: since=sub.subscription_expires_at or last_poll_cursor  # line 282, subscription_expires_at is wrong semantic — should be last poll timestamp

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `field_lww_applied_count` is computed by filtering for merge_rule == 'field_lww' (line 286), but the actual merge rule string set at line 249 is 'last_writer_wins_by_field_mtime'. The counter is always 0 due to string mismatch, breaking test I-05-COND112-04 which expects field_lww_applied_count==1.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: String literal mismatch makes a counter permanently 0, contradicting the spec and failing defined test scenarios.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Align string: either use 'field_lww' in both places or use 'last_writer_wins_by_field_mtime' in both.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: field_lww_applied_count = len([r for r in resolved if r.merge_rule == 'field_lww'])  # line 286; actual rule string is 'last_writer_wins_by_field_mtime' at line 249

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: `now` is only defined inside the 'create' CASE branch (line 146), but is referenced unconditionally in the 'get' case (`session.expires_at < now`, line 165) and 'refresh' case (line 174). NameError on get and refresh operations.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Undefined variable on two of the four primary operation paths (get, refresh) makes them unconditionally fail.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Move `now = current_timestamp()` to the top of execute() before the SWITCH, so all branches can use it.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: IF session.expires_at < now:  # line 165, inside CASE 'get'; now is only assigned inside CASE 'create' at line ~146

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Crisis detection (step 5) runs unconditionally, never checking `consent_flags['crisis_protocol']` which is declared in the input schema. The crisis_alert and ERROR event are emitted regardless of whether the user has consented to crisis protocol activation.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A declared consent flag that is never read means the consent mechanism is entirely bypassed — a fail-open security defect on a sensitive wellbeing feature.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `IF NOT request.consent_flags.get('crisis_protocol', False): SKIP crisis detection step` (or equivalent explicit gate) before computing crisis_score.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: crisis_score = compute_crisis_score(...)  # line 263, no check of consent_flags['crisis_protocol'] anywhere in step 5

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Distractor quality formula `quality = 1 - max(0, sim-0.7) * max(0, sim-0.3)` is broken: when sim < 0.3, the first factor max(0, sim-0.7)=0, making product=0 and quality=1.0, rewarding the worst (too-easy) distractors with maximum quality.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: The formula provably produces quality=1.0 for sim<0.3, which is the opposite of the documented intent ('too easy distractors should be penalized').
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Use a piecewise penalty function that applies separate penalties for sim<0.3 (too easy) and sim>0.7 (too similar), e.g. `quality = 1 - max(0, sim-0.7) - max(0, 0.3-sim)`.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: quality = 1 - max(0, sim-0.7) * max(0, sim-0.3)  # line 201; for sim<0.3: product=0, quality=1.0 (wrong — rewards easy distractors)

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Gate execution order is: policy_gate (step 1) → evidence_gate/token (step 2) → cost_gate/rate (step 3), violating LOCK-CD-07 which mandates policy_gate > cost_gate > evidence_gate. Cost gate at step 3 runs after evidence gate at step 2.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Violation of a declared execution-order LOCK is a contract defect — COND-110 and COND-111 implement the correct order; only COND-112 is inverted.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Reorder: step 2 = cost_gate (rate limit check); step 3 = evidence_gate (token refresh).
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: §4.1 게이트 실행 순서가 policy_gate(step1) → evidence_gate(token, step2) → cost_gate(rate, step3). LOCK-CD-07 정본 순서 'policy_gate > cost_gate > evidence_gate' 위반

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Module description declares 'distributed lock, duplicate prevention, retry on failure' as core features, but neither the algorithm nor Config contains any distributed locking, idempotency key, or job-level retry logic — these three stated core behaviors are entirely absent.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A cron scheduler without distributed locking and idempotency is guaranteed to produce duplicate job executions in multi-node deployments — correctness failure on the module's primary stated function.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add distributed lock acquisition (e.g., Redis SETNX) before job execution, an idempotency key per execution, and job-level retry logic with configurable backoff.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: 모듈 설명이 '실패 재시도, 중복 방지, 분산 잠금'을 핵심 기능으로 명시하나 알고리즘/Config에 분산락·중복방지(idempotency)·작업 재시도 로직 전무

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Module purpose per SOT #69 is Exponential Backoff with Jitter, max_delay, retryable-error classification, and DLQ. The E3 algorithm contains none of these; E10 config only has generic retry_policy(max_retries=2, backoff_ms=200) — no jitter, no max_delay, no DLQ path.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A retry-backoff module that implements neither exponential backoff nor jitter fails its entire stated purpose — correctness defect against the module's own contract.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Implement exponential backoff formula (`delay = min(max_delay, base_delay * 2^attempt)`), add jitter (`delay += random(0, delay*jitter_factor)`), classify retryable vs non-retryable errors, and add DLQ routing after max_retries.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: Module purpose per SOT #69 = Exponential Backoff, Jitter, max_delay, retryable-error classification, DLQ(dlq_count). E3 algorithm has none

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: request.memory_context.user_id is used directly as a memory namespace key (user_profile.id) across all memory tiers without any ownership or authorization check — enabling IDOR (insecure direct object reference) where caller A can read/write caller B's memories.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Missing tenant/ownership check on a multi-user memory store is an auth/authz bypass defect.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Before any store/recall/archive operation, verify that request.memory_context.user_id matches the authenticated principal from the session/token, and return COND_017_ACCESS_DENIED if they differ.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: user_profile = UserProfileStore.load(request.memory_context.user_id)  # line 189, no caller identity check

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: user_id from the request body is used directly to query personal memory (`memory_l0.search(request.user_id, ...)`) for knowledge_scope='personal' with no check that request.user_id matches the authenticated caller — enabling IDOR across user knowledge stores.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Missing authZ check on a personal knowledge access path is an auth bypass defect.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Validate that request.user_id matches the authenticated session principal before any L0/L1/L2 personal memory access.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: l0_results = memory_l0.search(request.user_id, query_vec, limit=3)  # line 206, user_id from request body, no auth check

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Config management module handles 'secret' writes (set/dynamic change) with no authorization check, no audit log, and no value validation in the algorithm. Permission is P0 (always active) and only policy+cost gates are mentioned.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Writing secrets without authZ is an auth bypass — any caller can set or overwrite secrets.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add an explicit RBAC/authZ check before any set/secret write operation, emit a mandatory audit event, and validate values against allowed types/patterns.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: 설정관리가 '시크릿' 저장·동적변경(set)을 다루나 set/secret 쓰기에 대한 authZ 점검·감사로그·값 검증 경로가 알고리즘에 없음

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Secret management module (get/set/rotate/audit) uses the same Permission Level P0 (always active) model as feature_flag — no RBAC, no PERMISSION_DENIED failure code, no enforced access-audit on get/set. Secret values flow through untyped `dict[str,Any]` payload with no schema enforcement or field-level encryption marker.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A secret management module with no access control is a critical auth bypass. Any module caller can get or set any secret.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Require caller_identity, secret ACL check, and mandatory audit log before dispatching any get/set/rotate operation. Define COND_051_ACCESS_DENIED. Use a typed SecretOperation schema instead of generic payload.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: Secret store (get/set/rotate/audit of API keys, passwords, certs) uses the SAME Permission/gate model as feature_flag: Permission Level P0 (기본 활성)

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: Audit-log 'write' (declared as append-only, tamper-proof, compliance-critical) maps backend-unavailable to fallback FB_COND_SKIP — silently dropping audit events with no buffering or durability guarantee when the backend is down.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: Silently skipping compliance-critical audit writes on failure is a data loss and security compliance defect — fail-open for an append-only log violates the core module contract.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Replace FB_COND_SKIP with a local durable buffer (e.g., WAL file) or at minimum return an explicit error to the caller instead of silently succeeding. Never skip audit writes.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote: Audit-log 'write' (append-only, 변조 방지, 규정 준수) maps backend-unavailable to fallback FB_COND_SKIP (skip)

2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.defect: revoke at line 197 calls `token_store.blacklist_access(meta.access_jti, ...)` but `save_refresh` at line 151 never persists the access token's jti — the field `meta.access_jti` does not exist in the saved refresh metadata. Revoke cannot blacklist the associated access token.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.why_objective: A token revocation that cannot blacklist the access token means the access token remains valid until natural expiry — a security defect.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.fix: Add `access_jti=access_claims['jti']` to the save_refresh call (line 151) so that revoke can retrieve and blacklist it.
2-2_COND-Modules-Detail.evidence_quote:
token_store.save_refresh(refresh_token, request.user_id, refresh_expires, refresh_count=0)  # line 151, no access_jti saved
token_store.blacklist_access(meta.access_jti, ...)  # line 197, meta.access_jti absent

When `speaker_challenge_audio` is None, `emb_sample` is undefined, causing NameError.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: `pcm` is assigned only inside the `if cfg.check_deepfake:` block (line 191 conditional). It is used unconditionally at line 235 `check_csam_audio_v2(pcm, ...)` and line 246 `chromaprint_fingerprint(pcm)` in CSAM and copyright checks. When check_deepfake=False, pcm is undefined — NameError crashes the safety pipeline.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: The deepfake weighted score formula (image×0.4 + audio×0.3 + lipsync×0.3) means a single-modality detection score of 1.0 can never exceed 0.4, which is below the 0.5 reject threshold. Integration test 2 in E8 explicitly confirms: 'image 0.8 → 0.32 → PASS'. A fully face-swapped video with no audio is never rejected.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: The design produces a provably reachable case where a clearly detected deepfake (individual score >= threshold 0.5) passes the safety gate — a correctness defect in a safety-critical pipeline.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Add a hard per-modality reject: if any individual score >= deepfake_threshold, trigger reject/manual_review regardless of weighted score.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.evidence_quote:
deepfake_score = (max_image_dfake * 0.4 +
                 max_audio_dfake * 0.3 +
                 max_lipsync_dfake * 0.3)
...
Test 2: image 0.8 + audio 0.0 + lipsync 0.0 → (0.8×0.4 = 0.32) → PASS

The true V1 for J-053/J-054 is multimodal_rag.md. This wrong baseline citation makes the entire V2 extension's provenance and diff-tracking incorrect.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Wrong V1 baseline reference is a provable specification lineage error — the V2 extends from a wrong parent, making the V1 baseline integrity claim false.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: Cache key uses `hash(query_embedding)` where `query_embedding` is `list[float]`. Python `list` is unhashable, causing TypeError at runtime. Even if converted to tuple, Python's built-in `hash()` is PYTHONHASHSEED-randomized per process, making exact cache keys non-deterministic across restarts — the exact-match cache never hits after a restart.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: TypeError is a provable runtime crash; even if workaround applied, cross-process hash inconsistency makes the cache correctness-broken.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Serialize the embedding to a deterministic bytes representation (e.g., struct.pack or json.dumps) and use a stable hash (xxhash64 or sha256) to form the cache key.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.evidence_quote: exact_hit = await cache_backend.get(f"{method}::exact::{hash(query_embedding)}")

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: The E3 engine dispatch only branches on 'nllb-200-local', 'deepl', and 'gpt-4o', but the config Literal also allows 'google' and 'claude'. If `cfg.default_engine` is 'google' or 'claude', variables `target` and `cost` are never assigned, causing NameError/UnboundLocalError at line 169 when building the return value.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: The schema declares valid values that the dispatch code does not handle, causing guaranteed runtime failure for those values.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Add `elif cfg.default_engine == 'google':` and `elif cfg.default_engine == 'claude':` branches, or raise NotImplementedError with a clear message, and add a final else clause.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: Cache key at line 115 includes `req.seed`, but `req.seed` may be None at that point — the actual seed is resolved at line 124 with `req.seed or random_seed()`. Two different requests with seed=None will collide on the same cache key, causing wrong cached results to be returned.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Resolve seed before cache key construction: `seed = req.seed or random_seed()` and use it in both the cache key and the generate call.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: Cache key at line 115 omits `negative_prompt`, `num_images`, `quality`, and `style_preset`. Two requests differing only in `negative_prompt` or `num_images` will collide on the same key and receive incorrect cached output (wrong negative prompt applied or wrong number of images returned).
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Cache key incompleteness causes provably incorrect results — functionally different requests return the same cached response.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Include all semantically significant parameters (negative_prompt, num_images, quality, style_preset, safety_strictness) in the cache key hash.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: generate_3d on EngineError at line 800 always falls back to ENGINES['triposr'] regardless of the original mode. TripoSR is an image-to-3d engine requiring an input image; for text-to-3d mode there is no image input — TripoSR fallback will fail immediately in text-to-3d mode.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: The fallback engine is mode-incompatible with text-to-3d requests — provably fails on a common input path.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Make the fallback mode-aware: use shap-e (local, text-to-3d capable) as fallback for text-to-3d mode, and triposr only for image-to-3d mode.

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: J-010 E3 calls `pil_load` (lines 959-960) before `scan_polyglot` (line 976). Malicious SVG with embedded JS or EXIF polyglot payloads are parsed by Pillow before the security scan runs — the scan is effectively bypassed for exploit payloads that act at parse time.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Parsing untrusted input before security scanning is a provable security ordering defect — any exploit that acts during parsing is not caught.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Move `scan_polyglot` and magic-byte sniff BEFORE any parsing call (PIL open, etc.).
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.evidence_quote: malicious = await scan_polyglot(req.image)  # line 976, after pil_load at line earlier

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: The semantic cache key contains only `method` and `query_embedding` — no `user_id`, `project_id`, or permission scope. One user's cached result can be returned to a different user with a semantically similar query, leaking private search results, RAG responses, or generated content.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Missing tenant isolation in a cache is a provable data-leakage security defect — cross-user result exposure.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Include user_id, project_id, and safety_policy_version in the cache key and in scan_recent filter criteria.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.evidence_quote:
exact_hit = await cache_backend.get(f"{method}::exact::{hash(query_embedding)}")
...
recent_keys = await cache_backend.scan_recent(method, limit=100)

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: AVRAGRequest and HybridSearchRequest include user_id, but the Qdrant search calls (visual_hits, text_hits, audio_hits) at lines 112-116 include no user_id filter. The privacy note requires user isolation, but vector searches return results from all users — cross-user data retrieval is possible.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Missing access control filter on a multi-tenant vector search is a provable tenant isolation defect.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Pass a `filter={"user_id": req.user_id}` parameter to every qdrant.search call and make it a required argument.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.evidence_quote:
visual_hits = await qdrant.search(f"{cfg.qdrant_collection}_visual", q_clip, limit=req.top_k * 2)
# no user_id filter

3-2_Multimodal-Processing.defect: auto_promote_to_l3=True promotes assets with importance >= 0.8 directly to L3 permanent storage without user consent. voice_chat.md (line 251-255) requires explicit user consent before permanent storage. The automatic promotion bypasses the mandatory consent gate.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.why_objective: Automatic permanent data storage without consent is a security/privacy defect — violates the declared consent requirement for L3 storage.
3-2_Multimodal-Processing.fix: Auto-promotion should only create a candidate; actual L3 write must wait for explicit user consent confirmation.

3-5_Education-Learning.defect: calculate_next_review() is declared as a synchronous `def` but contains `await self._schedule_cascade_review(dep_card_id)` at line 249. This is a Python SyntaxError/RuntimeError that makes the entire cascade review path non-functional.
3-5_Education-Learning.why_objective: await inside a non-async function is invalid Python syntax; the code cannot run at all, making cascade review a dead code path.
3-5_Education-Learning.fix: Declare calculate_next_review as `async def` and ensure all callers await it, or move the cascade scheduling out to an async wrapper caller.

3-5_Education-Learning.defect: `HintSystem.get_hint` references `self.HINT_LEVELS_PENALTY[level]` at line 392, but only `HINT_LEVELS` dict is defined in the class (line 373). `HINT_LEVELS_PENALTY` is never declared anywhere, causing AttributeError at every hint invocation.
3-5_Education-Learning.why_objective: Referencing an undefined class attribute always raises AttributeError; the hint system cannot produce a HintResponse under any code path.
3-5_Education-Learning.fix: Define `HINT_LEVELS_PENALTY = {1: 0.0, 2: 0.1, 3: 0.2, 4: 0.25, 5: 0.3}` (or equivalent) in HintSystem, or rename the reference to `HINT_LEVELS`.
