3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: ERROR_POLICIES defines 'node_timeout' with then='skip_with_default', but the handle() method only branches on policy.get('then')=='human_approval'. The 'skip_with_default' path is never executed; after max retries, a timeout falls through to else→fail instead.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: A documented policy that is never actually executed is a LOGIC defect causing wrong error behavior — not a preference.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Add an elif branch for policy.get('then') == 'skip_with_default' in the handle() method that skips the node and applies its default output.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: EventBus.subscribe() calls xack only after a successful handler return with no try/except. If handler(event) raises an exception, xack is skipped, the while True loop crashes, and messages remain in PEL (Pending Entry List) with no retry or DLQ mechanism — events are silently lost.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Exception in message handler causing permanent message loss with no retry is a MISSING_CRITICAL reliability defect on the event processing critical path.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Wrap handler(event) in try/except; on exception, either xack and log to DLQ, or use xclaim-based retry with a max-retry counter before DLQ.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
event = Event.deserialize(data)
await handler(event)
await self.redis.xack(stream_key, self.consumer_group, msg_id)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: desktop_automation.md and rpa_security_sandbox.md are declared as TODO/Phase 3 deferrals in the _index. LOCK-WF-10 mandates sandbox enforcement and file system access restriction for desktop RPA. The critical security specification for desktop RPA is entirely absent.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: LOCK-WF-10 makes sandbox enforcement mandatory; absence of the spec defining the sandbox boundary means no correct implementation is possible for desktop RPA.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Either author desktop_automation.md and rpa_security_sandbox.md before promoting desktop RPA to production, or explicitly scope desktop RPA out of the Phase completion gate.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: desktop_automation.md와 rpa_security_sandbox.md를 TODO로 선언한다 (06_desktop-rpa/_index.md)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: HumanApprovalNode on_timeout allows value 'approve', which causes the execution logic to auto-approve on timeout (decision = node.config.on_timeout). This silently bypasses the human approval gate — a critical authorization bypass.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Auto-approval on timeout means unanswered approvals are silently granted, defeating the purpose of the human gate; this is an auth bypass, not a preference.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Remove 'approve' from on_timeout allowed values. Timeout should produce decision='timeout' only; downstream nodes should handle escalation separately.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: Version management rollback and merge API endpoints accept user_id/created_by but the implementation performs no authorization or ownership check. Any caller can rollback or merge any other user's workflow without permission.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Missing authZ on mutating operations is a security defect (privilege escalation / unauthorized data modification).
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Add ownership/role verification before executing rollback and merge — only the workflow owner or authorized editors should be permitted.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
async def rollback(self, workflow_id: str, target_version: int, user_id: str) -> WorkflowVersion:
    target = await self._version_store.get(workflow_id, target_version)
    if not target:
        raise VersionNotFoundError(...)
    new_version = await self._create_version(...)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: DatabaseConditionSource.query is a raw user-supplied SQL string. The comment '(SELECT만 허용)' is the only enforcement mechanism — there is no parameterized query, SQL AST validation, or read-only connection enforcement. Malicious users can inject arbitrary SQL including DELETE/UPDATE/DROP.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Comment-only SQL injection prevention with no technical enforcement is a confirmed security defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Enforce SELECT-only via SQL AST parsing (e.g., sqlglot), use read-only DB connections, and parameterize all user-supplied values.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: query: string;                       // SQL 쿼리 (SELECT만 허용)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: _fetch_web_page and _fetch_api pass user-supplied source.url directly to httpx without any SSRF protection (no private IP range blocking, no scheme whitelist, no allowlist). Attackers can probe internal network endpoints including cloud metadata services.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Unrestricted SSRF vector via user-controlled URL is an objective security defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Validate URLs against an allowlist of domains or block private IP ranges (RFC1918, 169.254.0.0/16) before fetching; enforce https-only scheme.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=timeout) as client:
    resp = await client.get(source.url)
    resp.raise_for_status()

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: WebhookTrigger auth field allows 'none' with no restriction, meaning the publicly-exposed /api/v1/webhooks/{workflow_id} endpoint can trigger workflow execution with zero authentication. ip_whitelist=[] means all IPs are allowed.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: An unauthenticated external trigger endpoint for workflow execution is an objective security defect (fail-open on critical resource).
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Restrict auth='none' to local/dev environments only; require hmac_sha256 or api_key in production; set ip_whitelist to deny-all when empty or require explicit '*' to allow all.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
auth: "none" | "api_key" | "hmac_signature";
secret?: string;

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: TriggerEvent payload includes 'headers': dict(request.headers) — the full headers dict including X-Webhook-Signature-256 and X-API-Key auth headers. These propagate into EventBus, downstream workflow variables, and logs, exposing authentication credentials.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Persisting authentication credentials in event payloads is a secret exposure security defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Strip authentication, cookie, and signature headers before including headers in the payload. Use an explicit allowlist of safe headers to forward.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: VariableResolver passes env=context.environment_variables directly as a Jinja2 namespace, exposing all environment variables (API_KEY, DB_URL, etc.) to any template author via {{ env.* }}. No access control or masking is applied before rendering.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Unrestricted access to env secrets from workflow templates is an objective secret exposure defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Remove env from the Jinja2 context; require explicit SecretRef for secret access. If env must be included, use an allowlist of non-sensitive keys only.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
return jinja_template.render(
    nodes=context.node_outputs,
    vars=context.workflow_variables,
    env=context.environment_variables,
    global_=context.global_variables,

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: CodeNodeConfig has 'allowed_imports?: string[]' as optional with no default. If undefined means all imports are allowed (a common sandbox default), workflow authors could import 'os', 'subprocess', 'socket' to escape the sandbox. The spec does not define behavior when allowed_imports is absent.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Undefined-means-permissive on a sandbox import control is a security defect with potential sandbox escape consequences.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Set allowed_imports default to an explicit empty list [] (deny all) or define a safe default allowlist; document that omitting the field means deny-all.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: SecretStore.store_secret() calls self._aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None) with None as associated_data (AAD). Without AAD binding the ciphertext to the secret name/owner, an attacker who can write two entries can swap ciphertexts between them (confused deputy / secret substitution attack).
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Omitting AAD in AES-GCM allows ciphertext substitution attacks — a cryptographic security defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Pass a binding value as AAD, e.g., f'{name}:{owner_id}'.encode(), to both encrypt and decrypt calls.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: ciphertext = self._aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, None)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: State machine defines PAUSED state with transitions (RUNNING↔PAUSED, PAUSED→CANCELLED/TIMEOUT) but has no PAUSED→FAILED edge. An unrecoverable error while paused has no defined transition. Additionally, LOCK-WF-09 defines only PENDING→RUNNING→(SUCCESS|FAILED|CANCELLED|TIMEOUT) — PAUSED is not a legal stored status value per the LOCK.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Missing state transition for PAUSED→FAILED leaves the machine in a permanently stuck state on error; PAUSED stored as status directly contradicts LOCK-WF-09.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Add a PAUSED→FAILED transition for unrecoverable errors; store PAUSED as a separate control field (pause_state) rather than overwriting workflow.status to comply with LOCK-WF-09.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
| PAUSED | cancel_requested | CANCELLED | ...
| PAUSED | pause_timeout | TIMEOUT | ...
> **참고**: PAUSED는 RUNNING의 하위 상태로 간주. LOCK-WF-09 최종 상태는 SUCCESS/FAILED/CANCELLED/TIMEOUT 4종.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: MobileActionNodeConfig.action enum exposes only 10 values (missing 'keyboard' and 'mouse_move') but LOCK-WF-08 mandates exactly 12 actions. The file claims '12 액션 집합은 LOCK-WF-08 verbatim 유지' yet the schema contradicts this.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Schema exposes fewer actions than the LOCK mandates, making the LOCK-WF-08 compliance claim false and causing rejection of valid actions at the schema validation layer.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Add 'keyboard' and 'mouse_move' to the action enum in MobileActionNodeConfig to match LOCK-WF-08 exactly.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
action: "tap" | "swipe" | "type_text" | "screenshot" | "scroll"
      | "long_press" | "wait_element" | "ocr_extract" | "image_match" | "clipboard";

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: RetryPolicy uses backoff_type ('fixed'|'exponential') in Appendix A.2 (line 2009), but dag_architecture.md and error_handling.md use backoff_strategy ('fixed'|'exponential'|'linear'). Two different field names for the same schema field; 'linear' is also missing from the Appendix enum.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Schema field name mismatch between spec files causes deserialization failures and non-deterministic behavior in validators — a CONTRACT defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Standardize to backoff_strategy with values fixed/exponential/linear across all files; update Appendix A.2 accordingly.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
backoff_type: "fixed" | "exponential";   // Appendix A.2 L2009
vs.
backoff_strategy: "fixed" | "exponential" | "linear";  // dag_architecture.md, error_handling.md

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: execution_number uses 'count + 1 if config.max_executions else 0'. When max_executions is None (unbounded, the default), execution_number is hardcoded 0 for every fire, losing the cumulative count the schema describes as '누적 실행 횟수'.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Cumulative count always reporting 0 is a correctness defect in tracking logic, not a preference.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Track execution_number independently of max_executions; always increment from a persistent counter.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: "execution_number": count + 1 if config.max_executions else 0,

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: create_autofill_plan falls through from rule_based (confidence < 0.9) to LLM re-evaluation without re-appending a successful rule_based result. A rule_based match with 0.7≤conf<0.9 is silently discarded. Also, rule_based branch (line 223) appends FieldMapping without setting needs_confirmation, violating the FieldMapping contract.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Silently discarding a valid match and omitting a required contract field are correctness defects.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: For rule_based matches with 0.7≤conf<0.9, either accept them with needs_confirmation=True or explicitly fall through. Always set needs_confirmation in the rule_based branch per the FieldMapping contract.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: Variable syntax table defines global variables as '{{ global.<name> }}' but VariableResolver passes the context key as 'global_' (not 'global'). With StrictUndefined, any '{{ global.x }}' template will raise UndefinedError at runtime.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Template syntax mismatch with context key is a guaranteed runtime failure, not a preference.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Either change the Jinja2 context key from 'global_' to 'global', or update the documented syntax to '{{ global_.<name> }}'.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote:
| **전역 변수** | 전역 | 모든 워크플로우 공유 | `{{ global.<name> }}` |
...
global_=context.global_variables,

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: robots.txt processing references rate_limit.rps but RateLimitConfig defines the field as requests_per_second. The undefined attribute access will fail silently or raise AttributeError, making robots.txt crawl_delay enforcement dead code.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Referencing an undefined field name is a correctness defect — the crawl_delay enforcement will never execute.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Change rate_limit.rps to rate_limit.requests_per_second in the check_robots_txt pseudocode.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: rate_limit.requests_per_second = min(rate_limit.rps, 1/crawl_delay)

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: check_robots_txt has no error/fallback path for robots.txt fetch failure (network error, 404, malformed). The behavior on fetch failure (fail-open allowing scraping vs fail-closed blocking) is unspecified, a compliance-critical gap given robots_txt compliance defaults to true.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Undefined behavior on a critical compliance check is a correctness defect — fail-open would violate the compliance guarantee, fail-closed would break valid scraping targets with transient failures.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Define explicit behavior: on fetch failure, either fail-closed (BLOCKED with log) or use a configurable default. Document the chosen behavior in the spec.

3-4_Workflow-RPA.defect: ConditionTrigger and EventTrigger pass user-supplied 'expected' or 'threshold' values directly to re.search/re.match with no timeout, RE2 safe-engine, or pattern length limit. Complex user-supplied regex patterns can cause ReDoS (catastrophic backtracking).
3-4_Workflow-RPA.why_objective: Unbounded user-controlled regex execution is an objective denial-of-service security defect.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.fix: Apply a pattern length limit, use a safe regex engine (e.g., Google RE2), or enforce a per-evaluation timeout.
3-4_Workflow-RPA.evidence_quote: case "regex":    return bool(re.match(expected, str(actual)))

1-1_Verifier-Reasoning-Engines.why_objective: The asserted field does not exist in the schema. Every test using `verdict` will fail at runtime; the contract is internally contradicted.

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: E5/E7 spec mandates AUX-E-LIMIT-001 be raised for oversized input before entering the detect_format pipeline, but detect_format pseudocode (§4.2) has no size-check guard — the mandatory rejection path is absent.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The declared error path (size limit enforcement before processing) is a correctness requirement to prevent OOM/timeout on large inputs; its absence from the implementation pseudocode means the enforcement point is missing on the critical intake path.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Add a size check as the first statement in detect_format: `if len(raw.content) > MAX_SIZE: raise AuxError('AUX-E-LIMIT-001', ...)`
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote:
def detect_format(raw: RawInput) -> tuple[Modality, dict]:
    # 1. magic bytes (1순위...)
    magic = python_magic.from_buffer(raw.content[:512], mime=True)
    [no size check present]

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: The Secondary (GPT-4V) path has a single bare `except Exception` with no retry — a transient 429 or 503 on Secondary results in total failure with no retry attempt, while Primary gets 3 attempts with backoff.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The spec defines a fallback chain where Secondary is the last resort; a single transient error on Secondary causes permanent failure when one retry would succeed — a missing retry on the critical failure path.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Wrap the Secondary call in a retry loop (at least 1 retry with brief backoff) matching the documented fallback_chain policy.

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: WebAdapter sends the Tavily API key in the JSON request body (`json={'api_key': api_key, ...}`) rather than in an Authorization header, increasing key exposure surface in proxy/audit logs and request payloads.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: Embedding secrets in request bodies causes them to appear in server-side access logs, proxy logs, and debug captures in plaintext — a security exposure beyond what TLS alone mitigates.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Move the key to the Authorization header: `headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}'}` and remove it from the JSON body.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote: resp = await client.post("https://api.tavily.com/search", json={"api_key": api_key, "query": query, "max_results": 10})

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: Error code AUX-E-RENDER-023 ('grid_cols 무효, <1 or >5') is declared with sequential fallback in the error table (line 170), but render_composite pseudocode never validates `content.grid_cols` before passing it to `_grid_layout` — the declared error path is unreachable.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The documented error handling requires a validation gate that is entirely absent in the pseudocode; invalid grid_cols values will silently reach _grid_layout causing undefined behavior.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Add `if not 1 <= content.grid_cols <= 5: raise AuxError('AUX-E-RENDER-023', ...)` at the start of render_composite before the DAG construction.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote: | `AUX-E-RENDER-023` | grid_cols 무효 (<1 or >5) | YES | sequential fallback | [error table]; render_composite pseudocode has no grid_cols validation

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: `evaluate_promotion()` defines a 0.70 confidence gate but `distill()` never calls it; candidates are returned by confidence sort + top-5 only, so the promotion threshold is dead code and sub-0.70 candidates can be returned.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The promotion gate is a spec-required quality filter; its absence means the distillation output can include low-confidence memories that should be rejected, violating the stated threshold.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: In `distill()`, filter candidates with `candidate.confidence >= 0.70` before the sort+slice, or call `evaluate_promotion()` per candidate.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote:
distilled.sort(key=lambda c: c.confidence, reverse=True)
        return distilled[:5]

    async def evaluate_promotion(self, candidate: MemoryCandidate) -> bool:
        return candidate.confidence >= 0.70  # 임계값

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: `evaluate_and_trigger` uses sequential last-wins reassignment of `sdar_level` across triggers 1–5, but the spec (AUX-E-SDAR-003) requires the highest-severity level to be adopted. Any reordering or new trigger insertion silently regresses to lower severity.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The code relies on authored ascending-severity order of if-statements rather than computing the max; if triggers fire out of order or a new trigger is inserted at an unexpected position, the wrong (lower) SDAR level is emitted — a correctness defect relative to the AUX-E-SDAR-003 spec.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Compute the max: replace sequential assignment with `sdar_level = max(sdar_level or SDARLevel.AR_L1, new_level)` using the SDARLevel enum's natural ordering.

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: AUX-E-SEARCH-004 (top_k > 100 or < 1) is declared in the error table but never enforced in `search()`; line 154 slices `docs[:query.top_k]` directly, so top_k=0 or negative produces empty/wrong results without rejection.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: The declared validation gate is completely absent; top_k=0 produces an empty result silently, and negative top_k produces an empty slice — both are semantically incorrect and should raise AUX-E-SEARCH-004.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Add `if not 1 <= query.top_k <= 100: raise AuxError('AUX-E-SEARCH-004', ...)` at the start of `search()` before the cache lookup.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote: docs = sorted(docs, key=lambda d: d.score, reverse=True)[:query.top_k]

1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.defect: AR-L4 Emergency Kill Switch is delivered as a fire-and-forget event push (C-13) with no guaranteed-delivery, ACK, or retry; on failure only 'local queue 보존' (AUX-E005) — a security-critical action has no reliable delivery guarantee.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.why_objective: An Emergency Kill Switch is a security-critical control that must reliably reach its target; fire-and-forget delivery with no retry/ACK means a dropped event could leave a compromised pipeline running — a fail-open security defect.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.fix: Implement synchronous or at-least-once delivery for the Kill Switch: require an ACK from I-25 within a bounded timeout and retry on failure; escalate to a local emergency halt if ACK is not received.
1-2_Auxiliary-Modules.evidence_quote: AR-L4 ... C-13 fire-and-forget event push ... AUX-E005 | I-25 endpoint 응답 실패 — 로컬 큐에 보존

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: SSE idle timeout emits `final=true` with `state=working`. The `final=true` flag is defined as signaling stream termination with a terminal state, but `working` is non-terminal. Clients will misinterpret the task as completed and skip resubscription.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: The spec explicitly classifies `working` as non-terminal (task_lifecycle.md §2.2) and `final=true` as meaning the stream is done with a terminal state. Emitting both simultaneously is a self-contradiction in the spec that makes correct client implementation impossible.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Change the timeout close logic to emit `final=false` and a separate stream-closed signal (e.g., a reconnect event), or transition the task to `failed`/`canceled` before closing with `final=true`.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: streaming_sse.md line 150: 'event: task_status 마지막 이벤트에 final=true + state=working (미완료 경우) 로 종료 표시'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Push notification `task_status` defines `final=true` for `input-required` state. `input-required` is a non-terminal state in LOCK-A2A-02; setting `final=true` causes subscribers to close the task incorrectly.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: `input-required` is explicitly classified as non-terminal in task_lifecycle.md §2.2. Conflating it with terminal states in the `final` flag is a provable spec contradiction.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Restrict `final=true` to `completed|failed|canceled` only. Represent `input-required` with `final=false` and optional metadata.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: push_notifications.md line 155: 'state∈{completed, failed, canceled, input-required} 시 true'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Machine JSON Schema (§8) sets `required: ["jsonrpc", "method"]`, making `id` optional and allowing `result` and `error` to coexist in the same message. This permits invalid messages that mix request and response shapes.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: JSON-RPC 2.0 requires `id` in requests/responses and mandates mutual exclusivity of `result` and `error`. The schema allows structurally invalid messages to pass validation.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Split the root schema into `oneOf` variants: request (id required), notification (no id), success response (result only), error response (error only). Enforce mutual exclusivity of result/error.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: json_rpc_schema.md line 816: '"required": ["jsonrpc", "method"]' — id omitted; lines 842-843 allow both result and error fields simultaneously

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: `artifact.chunk` JSON-RPC method is defined in artifact_chunking.md but is absent from the canonical `A2AMethod` enum in json_rpc_schema.md. Any artifact chunk request is rejected as 'Method not found' by the schema.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: The canonical method enum exclusively controls which methods are valid. A method used operationally but absent from the enum breaks all conformant validators.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Add `artifact.chunk` to the `A2AMethod` enum in json_rpc_schema.md and the machine JSON Schema `method.enum` array.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: json_rpc_schema.md lines 830-838: method enum lists 8 entries, no `artifact.chunk`; artifact_chunking.md line 34: defines `artifact.chunk` as JSON-RPC method

3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: The canonical error_codes.md §4 C-1 unambiguously assigns -32001 to 'Task not found'. artifact_chunking.md independently reuses the same code for a different condition, breaking all client error-handling logic.

3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: Any component receiving these error codes from service_registry operations cannot determine which semantic applies, breaking CB logic, audit logging classification, and client recovery decisions.

Three documents disagree on the code.

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: SSE backpressure policy (§4.3 L163) silently drops `artifact_chunk` events when the in-memory queue is full, only incrementing a counter. artifact_chunking.md defines NACK/retransmit for chunk integrity. Silent drop breaks the end-to-end integrity guarantee.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: A dropped artifact_chunk causes the full artifact reassembly to fail (missing_indices will be non-empty, overall_hash will mismatch), but no NACK is sent so the sender doesn't know to retransmit. Data is silently lost.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Prohibit silent drop of artifact_chunk events. Instead apply backpressure to the sender, send an explicit NACK, or buffer in a persistent queue. Update §4.3 to distinguish artifact_chunk handling from heartbeat handling.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: streaming_sse.md line 163: '`artifact_chunk` 는 drop (데이터 손실 시 `task_status.metadata.dropped_artifacts` 카운터 증가)'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: `retryWithExponentialBackoff` increments `circuitBreaker.failure_count++` (L358) for every retry-eligible error, but §8.3 table defines only -32008, -32603, 408, 503, 401 as CB-applicable (YES). All other retry-eligible codes are marked NO for CB. The algorithm's behavior contradicts the classification table.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: The CB table in §8.3 is a prescriptive specification; the algorithm deviates from it by incrementing for codes that should not affect CB state. This causes premature circuit opening.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Add a `isCBEligible(code)` check before `failure_count++`, using the YES/NO classification from §8.3.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: error_codes.md line 358: 'circuitBreaker.failure_count++' (unconditional); §8.3 table lines 512-528: only -32008, -32603, 408, 503, 401 marked YES

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Delegation depth guard blocks at `delegation_depth ≥ 3` (L266), but LOCK-A2A-07 sets maximum depth = 3. A request at depth=3 is the last valid delegation call (depths 1→2→3 are all OK per delegation_chain.md §4.1); blocking at ≥3 prevents it.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: delegation_chain.md §4.1 explicitly states depth=3 is 'OK' and only depth=4 should be rejected. The guard's ≥3 condition is an off-by-one that rejects the last valid delegation.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Change the guard condition from `delegation_depth ≥ 3` to `delegation_depth > 3` (or equivalently `≥ 4`).
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: agent_selection.md line 266: '`SelectionRequest.delegation_depth ≥ 3` 시 `-32011` 에러 즉시 반환'; delegation_chain.md §4.1: 'depth = 3: 3단 위임 (OK, 본 토큰에서 추가 위임 발급 금지)'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Weight table adds `historical_success=0.05` to the four canonical factors (0.40+0.25+0.20+0.15=1.00) and asserts '합계 1.00'. The actual sum is 1.05. This breaks the normalized 0.0~1.0 total_score range.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: Arithmetic error in a specification document: 0.40+0.25+0.20+0.15+0.05=1.05 ≠ 1.00. Any score computed with these weights will exceed 1.0, invalidating thresholds (e.g., total≥0.7) defined elsewhere.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Renormalize the five weights to sum to 1.00 (e.g., reduce existing factors proportionally or set historical_success weight such that sum=1.00).
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: agent_composition.md lines 135-141: weights 0.40+0.25+0.20+0.15+0.05 with assertion '합계 1.00'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: `finalize(artifact_id, overall_hash)` is called by the sender (L129) and `expected_overall` is referenced in the receiver (L142), but no protocol message carries `overall_hash` from sender to receiver. The ArtifactChunk schema has no `overall_hash` field, so end-to-end integrity verification is impossible.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: The receiver cannot compute `expected_overall` without receiving it from the sender. The protocol is structurally incomplete — the integrity check it claims to perform cannot be executed.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Add `overall_hash: string` to the `finalize` protocol message schema, or include it in the last `ArtifactChunk` (e.g., `total_chunks` message). Define clearly when/how the receiver obtains this value.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: artifact_chunking.md line 129: 'finalize(artifact_id=aid, overall_hash=overall_hash)'; line 142: 'status = "ok" if sha256(reassembled) == expected_overall' — expected_overall source undefined

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: `issue_delegation_token()` sets `expires_at=parent_token.expires_at` (L277) without checking remaining TTL. If the parent token expires in 1 second, the newly issued child token will be immediately or near-immediately expired, making it unusable without detection at issuance time.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: Issuing a token with an already-expired or about-to-expire `exp` is a correctness defect — the child token cannot be used and the caller receives no indication at issuance time, causing silent auth failures downstream.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Before issuing a child token, validate that `parent_token.expires_at - now() ≥ min_delegation_ttl` (e.g., 60s). Reject issuance if the parent has insufficient remaining lifetime.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: delegation_chain.md line 277: 'expires_at=parent_token.expires_at' — no TTL remaining check before child token issuance

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: `issue_delegation_token()` code only validates `max_tokens` against the parent budget (L256), but the §5.1 invariant states all three fields must satisfy child ≤ parent: max_tokens, max_api_calls, AND max_cost_usd. max_api_calls and max_cost_usd are not validated in the guard code.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: A child token can be issued with max_api_calls or max_cost_usd exceeding the parent, violating the stated budget-inflation-denial invariant and allowing privilege amplification via resource budget.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Add guards for `budget.max_api_calls > parent_token.constraints.resource_budget.max_api_calls` and `budget.max_cost_usd > parent_token.constraints.resource_budget.max_cost_usd` in `issue_delegation_token()`.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: delegation_chain.md lines 255-257: only 'if budget.max_tokens > parent_token.constraints.resource_budget.max_tokens: raise ValueError'; §5.1 invariant lines 300-302 specifies all three fields must be ≤ parent

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Child token permissions are never validated to be a subset of parent permissions. `issue_delegation_token()` accepts any `permissions` list without checking `child.permissions ⊆ parent.permissions`. A delegate can obtain a child token with broader permissions than the parent.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: Privilege escalation through delegation is a security correctness defect. The `original_owner` invariant does not prevent a delegate from expanding their own permission scope when creating a sub-delegation.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Add `assert set(permissions) ⊆ set(parent_token.permissions)` in `issue_delegation_token()`. Raise -32012 or a new privilege-escalation error code on violation and emit an audit event.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: delegation_chain.md lines 233-279: issue_delegation_token() accepts permissions parameter without any subset validation against parent_token.permissions

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Fallback behavior for EXP_A2A_AUTH_FAIL → FB_A2A_RETRY: after 3 failed retries, task is FORCE_COMPLETE'd. Authentication failure should terminate to `failed`/`canceled`; forcing `completed` means an unauthenticated caller may receive success responses or partial results. FORCE_COMPLETE is also undefined in LOCK-A2A-02.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.why_objective: Marking a task as completed after authentication failures is a security defect: it bypasses the intended auth-failure outcome and may return results to unauthorized callers. The completed state also implies successful execution.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.fix: Replace FORCE_COMPLETE with a terminal `failed` transition using error code -32600 or an auth-specific error. Remove FORCE_COMPLETE from the fallback spec as it violates LOCK-A2A-02.
3-8_Conversation-A2A.evidence_quote: 구조화_종합계획서.md line 2743: 'EXP_A2A_AUTH_FAIL → FB_A2A_RETRY | 인증 실패 | 재시도 3회, 3회 초과 시 FORCE_COMPLETE'

3-8_Conversation-A2A.defect: Task lifecycle diagram in 상세명세 shows a `done` state (line 160) that does not exist in LOCK-A2A-02 six-state enum (`submitted|working|input-required|completed|failed|canceled`). Implementors may treat `done` as a distinct state.

3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.defect: Risk scenario 1 declares 'LOCK-BM-01 위반 발생' when V1 operational costs reach $44/mo. LOCK-BM-01 is defined as a user billing cap (사용자 과금 상한 ₩40,000/mo), not an operational cost limit. financial_projection.md explicitly states these are separate concepts.

3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.fix: Remove the 'LOCK-BM-01 위반 발생' label from the API cost scenario. Replace with the correct metric: if API cost increase forces end-user charges above ₩40,000, then LOCK-BM-01 is violated. Otherwise, this is purely an operational margin risk, not a LOCK violation.

3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.defect: The budget hardcap state machine has no defined atomicity policy for concurrent requests at the 95% threshold. A user's 'continue' action at 95% allows additional requests while async billing accumulates, potentially breaching the ₩40,000 LOCK-BM-01 hardcap with no rollback policy defined.
3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.why_objective: The spec defines a strict hardcap (LOCK-BM-01) but leaves the concurrent-request scenario unspecified. Any implementation that does not handle this race will silently violate the hardcap, which is a core product guarantee.
3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.fix: Add an atomicity clause: either (a) queue-and-check (block new requests until current billing is settled), (b) optimistic with rollback to local LLM if exceeded, or (c) reserve headroom. Document the policy in the state machine table.

3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.defect: LTV/CAC alert thresholds set WARN at '<5x' but the target is '≥3x', meaning any value 3x–4x (which meets the target) will permanently trigger WARN. A healthy 4x LTV/CAC will always be in alert state, making the WARN threshold useless for early warning.
3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.why_objective: An alert that always fires for healthy values is a correctness defect in the monitoring specification: it inverts the semantics of WARN (should indicate degradation approaching a threshold, not permanent alerting for in-target values).
3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.fix: Change WARN to '<3.5x' (or another value between 3x target and CRITICAL 3x threshold) so that WARN only fires as the metric deteriorates toward the CRITICAL boundary.

3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.defect: vamos_pricing_strategy.md §14 states 'Data Storage: 100% Local' and '당신의 데이터는 당신의 컴퓨터에만 존재합니다' as a core privacy guarantee. kpi_definitions.md §1.2 defines the MAU data source as '텔레메트리 서버 (opt-in 기반)', meaning user activity data is transmitted to an external server. 3-9_Business-Model-Strategy.fix: Qualify the privacy claim to 'content data is local-first; opt-in telemetry (activity counts, no content) is transmitted to a server for MAU measurement'. Update vamos_pricing_strategy.md §14 matrix accordingly and ensure opt-in consent flow is specified.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: rpc_protocol.md §7.3 text describes a 'stdin writer task: 별도 태스크가 mpsc stdin_tx 채널을 consume' and the diagram shows 'stdin_tx (mpsc) (writer task)'. But the PythonManager struct (§7.2) defines 'stdin: Arc<Mutex<Option<ChildStdin>>>' (direct Mutex) and the send() code (§7.4 L600-607) directly locks this Mutex to write. These two models are mutually contradictory: one is actor/channel-based, the other is lock-based.

An implementor following the text would create a different architecture than the code implies, leading to either broken mutual-exclusion or a redundant actor.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Remove the mpsc/stdin_tx description from §7.3 and the diagram if the Mutex approach is canonical, or add an actual stdin_tx mpsc channel field to PythonManager and convert the send() write path to use it.

HC is L1 in V1 but L2 in V2; Graceful is L3 in V1 but L4 in V2.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Remove the 5-layer §V2.4 table and replace it with a reference to the V1 §6 4-layer canonical table, or explicitly amend V1 §6 to accept 5 layers and propagate the renumbering across all cross-references.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: 상세명세 §A-2 declares conversation_stream returning Result<StreamHandle, TauriError> as an SSE streaming IPC command. But §G-1 in the same file explicitly states 'Tauri IPC는 단일 메시지 단위 (스트리밍 없음)'. The transport layer provably cannot support SSE streaming via Tauri IPC.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.why_objective: Tauri's invoke() is a single request-response call; it does not natively support SSE streaming. The command's return type StreamHandle implies a streaming channel that the declared transport cannot provide.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Either redesign conversation_stream to use Tauri events (tauri::AppHandle::emit_all) for streaming rather than a single invoke return, or document it as a future Phase 2 command and remove StreamHandle from the §A-2 signature.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: 상세명세 §D-1 Step 2 shows 'Command::new(python_path).args(["--json-rpc", "--port", "0"]).spawn()', implying a TCP socket idiom (--port 0 = OS-assigned port). 4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Remove '--port 0' from the spawn arguments in §D-1 and tauri_build_config.md §4.3 shell.sidecar scope. If a port argument is needed for future TCP mode, document it as a non-active Phase N placeholder.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: 상세명세 §D-1 Step 3 defines the Python ready signal as '{"ready": true, "pid": 12345}' (plain JSON object). rpc_protocol.md §3.2 defines the same signal as '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"_ready","params":{"pid":12345,"capabilities":[...]}}' (JSON-RPC 2.0 notification format). 4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Adopt rpc_protocol.md's JSON-RPC notification format as canonical (consistent with LOCK-RT-11 JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdout), update 상세명세 §D-1 Step 3, and ensure spawn_protocol.md parser handles _ready as a JSON-RPC notification.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: RpcResponse<R> has both 'result: Option<R>' and 'error: Option<RpcError>' as independent Option fields. The doc states the invariant is 'success or error (둘 중 하나)', but the struct allows both to be Some simultaneously or both to be None — neither the serde derive nor any constructor enforces the exclusive-one invariant.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.why_objective: A conforming JSON-RPC 2.0 response must have exactly one of result or error. A struct that allows both/neither violates this invariant at the type level, and a Python side that returns both fields populated will silently produce incorrect Rust-side behavior (the code at L620 checks error first, then unwraps result — if both are Some, the error is surfaced and result is dropped without warning).
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Replace RpcResponse<R> with an enum: 'enum RpcResponse<R> { Success { id: String, result: R }, Error { id: String, error: RpcError } }' or add a serde-level custom deserializer that enforces exactly-one validation.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.evidence_quote: rpc_protocol.md L149-156: 'pub result: Option<R>, ... pub error: Option<RpcError>' — doc comment L147: '성공 또는 에러 (둘 중 하나)'

Suspended is not in the canonical ProcessState enum, so no match arm exists for it in state-dependent logic.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.why_objective: An AtomicU8 state of 6 (Suspended) stored in ProcessState::Dead's match arm will be misidentified; every state comparison in the codebase that matches on ProcessState values will produce incorrect behavior for Suspended, since the variant does not exist in the enum.
4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Add 'Suspended = 6' to the ProcessState enum in rpc_protocol.md §7.2, update all match arms to handle the Suspended variant, and ensure the AtomicU8 comment is updated from '0=Idle...5=Dead' to '0=Idle...6=Suspended'.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: In §6.2 propagation table, the row 'L3 Graceful / shutdown RPC -32000' maps to 'T-02 (CrashExit)'. 4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Change the §6.2 row 'L3 Graceful / shutdown RPC -32000' to map to T-06 GracefulShutdownFailed, consistent with §3 T-06 definition.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.defect: IPC command files map rate_limit_exceeded to InternalError (message: 'rate_limit_exceeded'), but rpc_protocol.md §5.2 code -32013 maps rate_limit_exceeded to PermissionDenied via the From<RpcError> for TauriError implementation. A rate-limit condition produces two different TauriError variants depending on which layer surfaces it.

4-1_Rust-Tauri-Infrastructure.fix: Establish a single canonical TauriError variant for rate_limit_exceeded in AUTHORITY_CHAIN or plan §A.4 (PermissionDenied is more semantically correct), then update all command error matrices to match.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: Lines 384-391 use 'argocd app sync vamos-... --strategy canary' and '--strategy blue-green'. The 'argocd app sync' CLI command has no '--strategy' flag; progressive delivery strategies (canary, blue-green) are defined in Argo Rollouts CRDs, not as sync command arguments. These commands will fail with 'unknown flag: --strategy' at runtime.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: The '--strategy' flag does not exist in ArgoCD CLI 'app sync'; this is a verifiable API contract violation that causes immediate runtime failure of all k8s deployments using canary or blue-green strategies.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Remove the '--strategy' flag from 'argocd app sync'. Canary progression should be driven by 'kubectl argo rollouts promote' or Argo Rollouts analysis runs, not by the sync command.

The 1%-2% band is an undefined dead zone — error rate 1.5% fails the gate but does not satisfy the auto-rollback trigger. Same contradiction confirmed in runbook_prod_approval.md §3.4 and §5, and in CICD_PIPELINE_상세명세.md §F-2/F-3.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: A canary at 1.5% error rate simultaneously fails the deployment gate (auto-rollback required) and fails to satisfy the rollback trigger (>2% required). The system has no defined action for this state — a correctness gap in the deployment state machine.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Align the thresholds: define three explicit bands — <1% proceed, 1%-2% hold (manual decision), >2% auto-rollback — and apply them consistently across WF-7, runbook_prod_approval, and 상세명세.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: §5: '카나리 에러율 < 1% | 자동 | 자동 롤백' vs §6 L109: '카나리 에러율 > 2% | 자동 | 자동 롤백'

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: The rollback script at line 499 derives PREV as '$(docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml images --format json | jq -r '.[0].Tag')'. 'docker compose images' returns the CURRENTLY running (failing) image tag, not a previous good one. Rolling back to PREV restores the identical broken version.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: This is a provable logic inversion: the command used to determine the rollback target reads the currently-deployed (broken) image, not a prior image, making the rollback functionally a no-op.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Track the previous version before deployment begins (e.g., save to .env.prev or read from GHCR release history). Reference that saved value in the rollback script instead of querying the live container.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: PREV=$(docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml images --format json | jq -r '.[0].Tag')

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: The neo4j healthcheck at line 243 extracts the password via 'echo $$NEO4J_AUTH | cut -d/ -f2'. NEO4J_AUTH format is 'neo4j/<password>'; if the password itself contains '/', cut returns only the text before the second '/', silently producing a wrong password and causing healthcheck to always fail for any password with a slash.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: This is a provable logic defect: cut -d/ -f2 is not safe for passwords containing '/' — a valid character in randomly-generated credentials. Healthcheck failure blocks deployment incorrectly.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Use 'echo $$NEO4J_AUTH | sed 's|^[^/]*/||'' or set 'NEO4J_PASSWORD' as a separate variable to avoid parsing the composite credential format.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "cypher-shell -u neo4j -p $$(echo $$NEO4J_AUTH | cut -d/ -f2) 'RETURN 1'"]

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: Line 422 builds the overlay filename as 'docker-compose.${{ inputs.environment }}.yml'. The 'production' input (one of the two valid choices per lines 317-319) produces 'docker-compose.production.yml', but the canonical overlay defined at line 280 is 'docker-compose.prod.yml'. The file-not-found error will fail every production deployment.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: The overlay filename built from the workflow input ('production') does not match the defined overlay file ('prod'), making production deployments fail with a file-not-found error at the docker compose command. This is a correctness defect, not a preference.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Either rename the overlay file to 'docker-compose.production.yml', or add a mapping step: 'OVERLAY="docker-compose.$([ "${{ inputs.environment }}" = "production" ] && echo prod || echo staging).yml"'.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: OVERLAY="docker-compose.${{ inputs.environment }}.yml" ... # overlay files defined as deploy/docker-compose.staging.yml and deploy/docker-compose.prod.yml

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: The phase_b6 deploy workflow (lines 212-233) generates 'deploy/.env' on the GitHub Actions runner, then SSHes to the remote host to run 'docker compose up'. The .env file is never copied to the remote host. The remote '/opt/vamos/.env' is not updated with the new VAMOS_VERSION or secrets, so docker compose on the remote host uses stale or absent environment variables.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: The .env is created on the runner (local filesystem) but consumed by docker compose on the remote host (/opt/vamos). Without transfer, new secrets and the new VAMOS_VERSION never reach the deploy target — this is a provable logic defect in the workflow's data flow.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Transfer 'deploy/.env' to the remote host via 'scp deploy/.env user@$HOST:/opt/vamos/.env' before the SSH deploy step, or generate the .env content inside the SSH heredoc on the remote host directly.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: Generate .env file: 'cat > deploy/.env <<EOF ... EOF' (runner local) → Deploy via SSH: 'ssh ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }} << ENDSSH cd /opt/vamos && docker compose up' (no scp/rsync step between them)

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: cache_strategy_detail §6.2 and CICD_PIPELINE_상세명세 §H-2 both specify that WF-13 nightly performs 'actions/cache/delete + warm' every Sunday. However, WF-13_nightly.md line 65 explicitly states 'nightly 는 캐시 무효화나 override 를 수행하지 않으므로 별도 캐시 섹션 없음'. The weekly cache purge is fully unimplemented.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: The cache strategy specification documents a weekly purge as a required correctness mechanism (preventing stale cache poisoning); WF-13 explicitly omits it. This is a documented behavioral guarantee with no implementing workflow.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Add a cache-purge job to WF-13_nightly.md that calls actions/cache/delete for all defined cache keys, then re-runs cache warming, consistent with cache_strategy_detail §6.2.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: cache_strategy_detail §6.2: '매주 일요일 | 전 캐시 | WF-13 nightly → actions/cache/delete + warm' vs WF-13_nightly.md: 'nightly 는 캐시 무효화나 override 를 수행하지 않으므로 별도 캐시 섹션 없음'

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: WF-5's secrets table at §7 (lines 117-126) does not list TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY or TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD. The create-release job performs Tauri Updater signing, and runbook_code_signing.md §3.4 explicitly states these two secrets were 'missing from WF-4_build-tauri.md §5.1 / WF-5_release.md §7'. Implementers reading WF-5 alone will omit the signing keys.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: Without TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY, the Tauri auto-updater cannot verify update signatures, causing all client update installations to fail. The secrets table is the authoritative implementation reference; its omission is a critical missing specification.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Add TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY and TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD as required entries to WF-5_release.md §7 secrets table and WF-4_build-tauri.md §5.1, consistent with runbook_code_signing §3.4 and secrets_mapping #29/#30.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: k8s_argocd_pipeline.md line 378 uses 'secrets.ARGOCD_AUTH_TOKEN' in the deploy workflow. This secret is completely absent from secrets_mapping.md — it is not in the master table, not listed as a V3 Stub, and not mentioned anywhere in the secrets rotation policy. KUBECONFIG is acknowledged as a Stub, but ARGOCD_AUTH_TOKEN has no entry at all.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: A secret used in a production deployment workflow that is absent from the rotation/access-control registry creates an unmanaged credential — it will have no defined rotation schedule or owner, and implementers cannot provision it from the specification.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Add ARGOCD_AUTH_TOKEN to secrets_mapping.md as a V3 required secret with rotation period (recommend 90 days), owner (platform team), environment scope (production), and used-by (deploy-v3.yml argocd-sync job).

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: macOS keychain is created and unlocked with the hardcoded literal password 'ci' on lines 124-128. Though the runner is ephemeral, any process on the runner during the build window can exploit a known credential to read the Apple Developer ID private key from the keychain.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: Hardcoded credential directly enables exfiltration of the Apple Developer ID certificate; standard practice and security guidance require a per-run random password. This is a factual secret-exposure defect, not a style preference.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Replace the literal 'ci' with $(openssl rand -base64 32) stored in a variable before the create-keychain call; pass that variable to unlock-keychain and set-key-partition-list.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote: security create-keychain -p "ci" build.keychain ... security unlock-keychain -p "ci" build.keychain ... security set-key-partition-list -S apple-tool:,apple: -s -k "ci" build.keychain

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: The prod ArgoCD Application manifest (lines 114-118) has 'syncPolicy.automated:' block present with only 'selfHeal: false'. In ArgoCD, an 'automated:' block (even with selfHeal disabled) causes ArgoCD to auto-sync when the git source changes. The update-helm-values job (lines 353-359) auto-commits a new image tag to main; ArgoCD will then auto-sync the prod deployment without requiring the GitHub Environment 2-person approval gate that guards only the argocd-sync job (line 364), bypassing LOCK-CI-10.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: ArgoCD's automated sync is triggered by git changes regardless of selfHeal setting; selfHeal only controls sync in response to cluster drift. The 2-person approval gate on argocd-sync is circumvented by the preceding commit step. This is an auth/authz bypass, not a preference.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Remove the 'automated:' block entirely from the prod Application manifest (set it to false) so all prod syncs require explicit 'argocd app sync' invocation through the gated job. Staging can retain automated sync.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.evidence_quote:
syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: false
      allowEmpty: false

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: Lines 399-408 write POSTGRES_PASSWORD, QDRANT_API_KEY, NEO4J_AUTH, and OPENAI_API_KEY in plaintext to 'deploy/.env' in the runner workspace. No chmod, cleanup, or artifact-exclude procedure is documented. secrets_mapping.md §8 declares that log bodies must never contain actual secret values, but does not address the plaintext .env file persistence risk.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: Writing raw secrets to a workspace file without documented cleanup or exclusion from artifact uploads is a secret-exposure defect; GitHub Actions artifacts can expose workspace files if misconfigured.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Add 'chmod 600 deploy/.env' immediately after creation; add a post-deploy step 'rm -f deploy/.env'; explicitly exclude deploy/.env from any artifact upload steps.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: Y-18 in phase_b6 (§3.3 L112, §2 L70) specifies 'gitleaks' as the secret-leak scanning tool, while WF-8_security-scan §3 L49 and secrets_mapping §8.1 L293 specify 'trufflehog'. LOCK-CI-08 canonical 5-tool list also specifies trufflehog. The two tools have different detection capabilities; using the wrong tool creates a security gap.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.fix: Align phase_b6_yaml_normalization.md Y-18 to reference trufflehog, consistent with LOCK-CI-08, WF-8_security-scan, and secrets_mapping.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: CICD_PIPELINE_상세명세.md L393 describes build-tauri.yml as '3-platform 병렬', but LOCK-CI-06 (canonical authority via AUTHORITY_CHAIN.md) mandates '4플랫폼 (Linux x64, Windows x64, macOS ARM64, macOS x64)'. The same document self-contradicts the LOCK it is supposed to implement.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: Critical CVE severity has conflicting PagerDuty priority assignments in the same document: §4 L52 assigns Critical to 'P0 incident', §4.1 L61 assigns Critical to 'PagerDuty P1', and §7 L86 again assigns Critical to 'PagerDuty P1'. P0 and P1 route to different on-call queues; the discrepancy makes the incident response path for Critical CVEs ambiguous.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: P0 and P1 are distinct PagerDuty escalation levels with different response SLAs and on-call teams. A document-internal contradiction means on-call engineers cannot determine the correct escalation path, a correctness defect in the incident response state machine.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: benchmark_baseline.md workflow_run trigger at line 110 listens for workflow named 'Build Tauri App', but WF-4_build-tauri.md line 26 defines the actual workflow name as 'Build Tauri'. GitHub's workflow_run trigger matches on the exact 'name:' field; 'Build Tauri App' will never match, silently excluding all WF-4 runs from baseline metrics.
4-2_CICD-Pipeline.why_objective: GitHub Actions workflow_run triggers are exact-string matched on the workflow name field. A mismatch silently causes the trigger to never fire for WF-4, creating a systematic gap in baseline data collection — a correctness defect, not a naming preference.

4-2_CICD-Pipeline.defect: CICD_PIPELINE_상세명세.md §H-1 L482 defines the Tauri cache key as 'tauri-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}' with no hash input. cache_strategy_detail §3.2 defines the canonical Tauri key as 'tauri-target-${{ runner.os }}-${{ matrix.target }}-${{ hashFiles('Cargo.lock') }}'. Without the Cargo.lock hash, the 상세명세 key never invalidates when dependencies change, serving stale Tauri build artifacts.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: acquire_half_open_slot() references cb._half_open_in_flight, but this field is not declared in the CircuitBreaker dataclass (§5.3). Once set to True, _trip()/_close()/probe_if_due() never reset it, so after the first HALF_OPEN probe the slot is permanently blocked and CLOSED recovery is impossible.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: The undeclared field causes AttributeError on first access or, if Python allows dynamic attributes, a permanent latch that prevents circuit-breaker recovery — a correctness failure on every server outage after the first.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Add `_half_open_in_flight: bool = False` to the CircuitBreaker dataclass, and reset it to False in _close() and _trip().
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.evidence_quote: if cb.consecutive_successes == 0 and cb._half_open_in_flight:  ...  @dataclass class CircuitBreaker: ... (no _half_open_in_flight field)

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: execute_with_retry() checks `cb.state == 'OPEN'` before every attempt but never calls cb.probe_if_due(). Without probe_if_due(), the CB can never transition OPEN→HALF_OPEN; the 60-second recovery window is unreachable dead code.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: Once a circuit breaker trips to OPEN, it can never automatically recover; every subsequent call fast-fails indefinitely, permanently disabling the server in the bridge.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Call `cb.probe_if_due()` immediately before the OPEN check so that after 60s the state transitions to HALF_OPEN and a probe attempt is allowed.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.evidence_quote: if cb.state == "OPEN":  return fast_fail(ctx, cb)  (no call to cb.probe_if_due() anywhere in execute_with_retry)

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: connection_pool.md §2.2 slot model has slot #10 reserved for 'Phase 3 Sentry OR Exa', but external-servers _index confirms both Sentry and Exa are approved (11 servers total in Phase 4). The pool can accommodate only one of the two simultaneously.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: The pool slot model has an off-by-one defect relative to the current server count; one of the two approved servers has no slot definition and cannot be registered in the pool.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Expand pool slot model to at least 11 slots (or redesign to dynamic allocation), explicitly mapping slots for both Sentry and Exa.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: F-2 file_write schema (the Phase 0 canonical schema) lacks the allowlist path-matching and path-traversal protection defined in A-6 (L167-168). F-2 also adds create_dirs:true enabling arbitrary directory creation, which exacerbates the missing protection.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: The security control (allowlist + path traversal blocking) is present in one schema version but absent from the canonical one; implementations built from F-2 will not enforce path restrictions.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Add to F-2's schema description: allowlist path matching against configured directories, rejection of '..' path traversal, and rejection of executable writes. Gate create_dirs:true behind the same allowlist.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: Multiple external MCP server runtime commands use @latest (e.g., '@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@latest') without version pinning or integrity hashing. marketplace_security_sop.md §2 #2 requires supply-chain/package-integrity scan and blocks unsigned/tampered packages, but the runtime execution commands bypass this by using floating version references.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: Using @latest makes builds non-reproducible and allows a compromised package version to be silently executed; the supply-chain SOP requirement and the runtime command are directly contradictory.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Pin all external MCP package versions to exact semver + lockfile/integrity hash. Remove -y (auto-install) npx flags in production config, or enforce version-pinning via CI policy.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: Error code -3 is assigned to two different categories: 'invalid_response' (§3.3.3 table row 6 / L234) and 'payload_too_large' (§3.3.1 code / L243, §7.2 #a). Downstream code branching on the numeric code cannot distinguish the two.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: A single error code cannot unambiguously identify two distinct error categories; callers using code-based dispatch will misroute one of them.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Assign a distinct code to payload_too_large (e.g., -4) and update §7.2 #a and all downstream references.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: fast_fail() emits McpError(code=-2, category='server_error'). The error catalog §3.4.1 defines code -2 as 'timeout' and 'server_error' as 5xx/JSON-RPC -32000. Using code=-2 for a server_error category violates the catalog's own code-to-category mapping.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: Consumers that use the numeric code to route errors will mis-classify a CB-open event as a timeout, potentially applying timeout retry semantics instead of CB-open semantics.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Assign fast_fail a code consistent with server_error (e.g., use the 5xx range or a dedicated -5 constant) and update §7.2 accordingly.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: web_search result-limit parameter is named 'max_results' in A-7 (L174) but 'count' in F-3 (L533) for the same tool. A-7 also has 'region' while F-3 has 'language'/'freshness'. Callers using one schema will send unrecognized fields to an implementation built from the other.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: Field name mismatch means one schema is a breaking interface change relative to the other; client code written against A-7 cannot interoperate with server code written against F-3.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Unify to one parameter set (F-3's count/language/freshness appears more complete); deprecate A-7's region/max_results.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: Fail-closed fallback allows per-instance local counter of 67/day. With N instances all losing Redis, the aggregate is N×67/day, which can exceed the 2000/month shared quota (67×30=2010 even for a single instance).
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: The quota protection mechanism is bypassed under the specific failure mode (Redis partition) it is designed to handle; the quota ceiling 2000/month cannot be enforced.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Cap the local fallback at a smaller value that is safe under worst-case N instances, or fail-open (allow) rather than fail-closed with a per-instance counter that sums above the monthly ceiling.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.evidence_quote: Redis 연결 실패 시 보수적 차단(fail-closed) — 로컬 폴백 카운터로 인스턴스당 67/day 임시 상한 적용 후 재연결 시 reconcile

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: acquire() triggers pool_exhausted at active=5 (LOCK-MCP-05, max simultaneous servers), not at the pool max_connections=10 (LOCK-MCP-10). A pool of 10 slots is functionally capped at 5, making 5 slots permanently unused.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: The acquire logic conflates two separate LOCK constraints (active servers vs pool connection slots), resulting in premature exhaustion errors.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Separate the LOCK-MCP-05 (max active servers=5) check from the pool slot limit (LOCK-MCP-10 max_connections=10). Exhaustion should trigger only when all 10 pool slots are occupied, not at active servers=5.

4-3_MCP-Server-Client.defect: The 95%-quota gate blocks requests with priority ∈ {low, medium}, but §3.3.3 defines the default priority as 'medium'. All callers omitting priority are blocked at 95% consumption, making the gate far more aggressive than intended.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.why_objective: Combining a default of 'medium' with a gate that blocks 'medium' means standard unannotated traffic is cut off, defeating the purpose of a high-priority-only gate.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.fix: Change the default priority to 'low', or change the 95% gate to block only 'low', or change the gate to block nothing and reserve it for 100% only.
4-3_MCP-Server-Client.evidence_quote: 95% 소진 | ... priority ∈ {low, medium} 차단, priority=high 만 허용 ... §3.3.3 priority ('low'/'medium'/'high', 기본 'medium')

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: insert_run() atomicity spec (L1318): Parquet is written first, then SQLite INSERT; on SQLite failure the Parquet file is unlinked. But if the process crashes between the Parquet write and the SQLite INSERT (power loss, OOM, SIGKILL), the Parquet file remains as an orphan with no SQLite row, and no cleanup path is described for this crash scenario.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: An orphaned Parquet file without a corresponding SQLite row corrupts the storage invariant; subsequent diff_runs or get_per_item_results calls will silently skip the data, leading to missing benchmark history.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Add a startup orphan-cleanup routine that scans benchmark_results/parquet/ for Parquet files with no matching SQLite run_id and removes them; or use a write-ahead log / two-phase commit pattern to guarantee atomicity across crash.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L1318: "insert_run 원자성 확인: Parquet 쓰기 먼저 → SQLite INSERT → SQLite 실패 시 Parquet 파일 정리(unlink). 구조적 보장"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: T-03 test scenario (L648) applies Cohen's Kappa ≥ 0.6 to a test-retest scenario where the same judge evaluates the same response twice. Cohen's κ is an inter-rater agreement statistic requiring ≥2 independent raters; applying it to a single rater's repeated scores misidentifies its meaning and may mask real inconsistency.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: Cohen's κ is mathematically undefined or trivially 1.0 for a single rater comparing to themselves; applying it to single-rater test-retest is the wrong statistical test for the intended measurement of judge consistency.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Replace Cohen's κ with intra-class correlation (ICC) or a score-delta threshold (e.g., |score_run1 − score_run2| ≤ 1.0) for the single-judge test-retest scenario.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L647-648: "| T-03 | LogicKor judge 일관성 (같은 응답 2회 평가) | 동일 응답에 대해 judge 2회 실행 | 점수 차이 ≤ 1.0점, Cohen's Kappa ≥ 0.6 |"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: S7G-065 VBS-7 Constitution threshold states '위반 ≤ 3/1000 (준수율 ≥ 95%)'. A violation rate of 3/1000 = 0.3% implies compliance of 99.7%, not 95%. The two sub-expressions cannot be simultaneously satisfied and produce contradictory pass/fail outcomes depending on which is used.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: The threshold pair is mathematically self-contradictory: at exactly 3/1000 violation rate, the compliance rate is 99.7%, not 95%; an implementation using 95% compliance as the gate will apply a far weaker standard (50/1000 violations) than intended.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Decide which is authoritative and correct the other: either '위반 ≤ 3/1000 (준수율 ≥ 99.7%)' or '위반 ≤ 50/1000 (준수율 ≥ 95%)', and update the LOCK entry accordingly.

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: S7G-082 '시나리오 기반 테스트' is registered in 04_human-evaluation/_index.md as DONE/V2 but no dedicated design file exists. crowd_eval.md L125 explicitly states 'S7G-082 시나리오 기반 테스트는 본 파일 비대상 (별도 채널)'. No file in the 04_human-evaluation directory implements this benchmark.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: An item declared DONE in the index with no implementing file means the benchmark will never run; the coverage claim of 88 S7G items DONE is false for this item.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Create a dedicated S7G-082 implementation file under 04_human-evaluation/ (or explicitly delegate to an existing file with a clear pointer), specifying scenario list, measurement criteria, pass/fail threshold, and execution cadence.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: crowd_eval.md L125: 'S7G-082 시나리오 기반 테스트는 본 파일 비대상 (별도 채널)'  _index.md L17: S7G-082 status listed as V2. Grep confirms only _index.md and crowd_eval.md mention S7G-082.

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: LOCK-BE-06 / §B.4.4 (L3505) defines B=10000 for n<100 and B=5000 for n>1000, leaving the range 100≤n≤1000 completely undefined. Implementations in rag_benchmarks.md apply B=5000 for n=300 (using 'n>100' rule) but LOCK-BE-06 has no such rule, creating arbitrary divergence.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: An undefined gap in the bootstrap resample specification causes each benchmark to choose B arbitrarily for medium-sized datasets (100≤n≤1000), making confidence intervals incomparable across benchmarks.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Add the missing interval rule to LOCK-BE-06, e.g., 100≤n≤1000 → B=10000 (or B=5000), and update all benchmark files to use the same rule.

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: F-05 산출물 tags field is dict (confirmed at L1329), but F-02 BenchmarkResult schema defines tags as array[string] (L659). The plan acknowledges this with 'dict fallback 모드로 정상 적재·조회' but accepts schema violation permanently rather than correcting it.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: A Pydantic schema contract violation that is silently swallowed in fallback mode means any consumer of BenchmarkResult expecting list[str] tags will receive an unexpected dict, causing runtime type errors or silent data misinterpretation.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Either migrate F-05 tags output to list[str] to match F-02 schema, or formally update F-02 schema to accept dict[str, Any] for tags and version-bump the schema; eliminate the undocumented fallback.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L1329: "F-05 산출물 tags 필드 호환 확인: F-05의 tags가 dict 형태(F-02 스키마는 list[str])로 Pydantic 역직렬화 시 경고 발생 → dict fallback 모드로 정상 적재·조회"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: S7G-048 BBQ header states '11 bias 카테고리' (L24) but the category list on L25 enumerates only 10 (age, disability, gender, nationality, physical, race, religion, SES, sexual, two_intersect). The 11th standard BBQ category is absent.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: The count claim '11' is inconsistent with the actual enumerated list of 10; the missing category will never be tested, leaving a bias blind-spot that the spec claims to cover.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Add the missing 11th BBQ category (e.g., race_x_gender or race_x_SES intersectional category) to both the list and the evaluation loop, or correct the count to 10.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L24: "11 bias 카테고리"  L25: "카테고리: age, disability, gender, nationality, physical, race, religion, SES, sexual, two_intersect" (10 items listed)

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: S7G-046 Prompt Injection weighted-average weights (direct .30/indirect .20/jailbreak .20/roleplay .15/multilingual .15) do not correspond to dataset sample sizes (150/100/100/80/70 = .300/.200/.200/.160/.140). Roleplay is up-weighted (.15 vs .16) and multilingual over-weighted (.15 vs .14), skewing the aggregate defense rate.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: Misaligned weights cause the aggregate defense rate to diverge from the actual sample-proportionate performance; a model could pass the aggregate gate while failing on the proportionally largest attack categories.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Align weights to actual dataset proportions: direct 0.30, indirect 0.20, jailbreak 0.20, roleplay 0.16, multilingual 0.14, or document the intentional weighting rationale and update the LOCK.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L267-272: "weights: direct: 0.30 / indirect: 0.20 / jailbreak: 0.20 / roleplay: 0.15 / multilingual: 0.15"  L231-237 dataset: Direct 150, Indirect 100, Jailbreak 100, Role-play 80, Multilingual 70

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: The '종합 (가중)' κ = 0.71 is arithmetically incorrect. Sample-weighted mean of session κ values: (30×0.78 + 30×0.74 + 300×0.68) / 360 = 249.6/360 = 0.693. Neither sample-weighted (0.693) nor simple mean (0.733) equals 0.71.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: A reported κ that cannot be derived from the session-level data is an internal arithmetic contradiction; downstream LOCK-BE-05 gate (κ ≥ 0.6) and CI reporting rely on this value.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Recalculate the composite κ using the correct sample-weighted formula or provide a transparency note explaining the pooled estimation method that yields 0.71; update the CI accordingly.

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: RC-16 checks all benchmark_results.values() for the presence of confidence_interval, but the same dict contains entries like 'MT-Bench-Judge', 'LogicKor-Judge', and 'PromptInjection' which have no bootstrap CI. RC-16 will spuriously FAIL on every release, and RC-17's len(benchmark_results) ≥ 13 is inflated by the same non-benchmark entries.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: RC-16 will always fail because judge entries are mixed into the benchmark_results dict without CI fields; this makes the release gate permanently broken or forces workarounds that bypass the check.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Separate benchmark entries (with CI) from judge/meta entries in the benchmark_results dict, or filter RC-16 to only check entries where CI is expected (e.g., by benchmark type flag); correct RC-17 count to exclude non-benchmark entries.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L180-184: "checks['RC-16'] = { 'passed': all(hasattr(r, 'confidence_interval') and r.confidence_interval for r in benchmark_results.values()) }"  L173: "injection_result = benchmark_results.get('PromptInjection')"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: §A.3 priority table (L3330-3333) states HIGH=2건 LLM보조, MEDIUM=2건 수동, LOW=2건 수동 (6 non-auto total), but §A.4 (L3346) declares Manual (LLM 보조 포함) = 2. The actual catalog has 3 non-auto rows (T-097 Manual, T-185 ⚠️LLM, T-188 ⚠️LLM). Two different totals (6 vs 2 vs 3) for the same count are internally contradictory.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: Inconsistent automation counts mean the stated 97% automation rate (L3334) is unreliable; implementers have no consistent basis for knowing how many manual test runs are required.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Audit actual test catalog automation markers and reconcile §A.3 text descriptions and §A.4 table to agree on a single non-auto count.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L3330-3333: "HIGH | ... | 98% 자동 (2건 LLM 보조) / MEDIUM | ... | 96% 자동 (2건 수동) / LOW | ... | 93% 자동 (2건 수동)"  L3346: "Manual (LLM 보조 포함) | 2"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: RC-13 checks only that regression_report['critical_regressions'] == 0 (L161), relying entirely on an upstream computation. There is no validation that the upstream computation applied LOCK-BE-14's 1% CRITICAL threshold (vs the 3% general threshold). An upstream bug mis-applying 3% to CRITICAL items would silently pass RC-13.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: The gate logic for the highest-severity regressions provides no in-checklist verification that the CRITICAL 1% threshold was applied; a 2% regression on a CRITICAL benchmark would pass undetected.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Add an explicit threshold check in RC-13: directly compute critical_regression_count from benchmark_results using LOCK-BE-14 thresholds (1% for CRITICAL items, 3% for others) rather than trusting a precomputed key.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L158-163: "# RC-13: 회귀 없음\n ... 'passed': regression_report.get('critical_regressions', 0) == 0"  LOCK-BE-14: "CRITICAL 항목은 1% 하락 시 알림"

5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.defect: LogicKor A-4 (L61-69) specifies GPT-4 as judge with a 1-10 scale but provides no judge prompt template, no pinned model version/snapshot, no cost controls, no rate limiting, and no fallback if the GPT-4 API is unavailable. LLM-as-judge results are highly sensitive to prompt wording and model version drift.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.why_objective: Without a pinned judge prompt template and model version, benchmark results will be non-reproducible across runs — directly violating R-18-1 (reproducibility mandate) and making LOCK-BE-03 (≥ 8.0/10) gate comparisons meaningless across different judge versions.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.fix: Add to A-4: (1) a verbatim judge prompt template, (2) a pinned model identifier (e.g., gpt-4-0125-preview), (3) an API fallback strategy, and (4) cost/rate-limit controls; register in LOCK-BE-03.
5-1_Benchmark-Evaluation.evidence_quote: L61-69: "**평가 방식** | GPT-4 심판 (judge) + 인간 평가" — no prompt template, model version, fallback, or cost control specified.

5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.fix: Assign a new LOCK in domain #9 Health-Wellness (e.g. LOCK-HW-13) for CBT 15 distortion types, then update the LOCK inheritance in cognitive_distortion_detector.md and thought_record.md to reference the correct LOCK ID.

5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.defect: §3.2 states 'this folder defines no new LOCKs' and §3.4's absolute rule states 'self-defined LOCKs are prohibited', but the same §3.4 table and AUTHORITY_CHAIN both define LOCK-V12-01 and LOCK-V12-10 as self-defined LOCKs. The governance rule directly contradicts its own implementation.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.why_objective: The authority rule ('자체 LOCK을 정의하지 않는다') is violated within the same document that declares it. Any LOCK-change protocol or validator reading this rule will conclude the 2 self-LOCKs are unauthorized.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.fix: Amend the absolute rule to 'only LOCK-V12-01 and LOCK-V12-10 are permitted as self-defined LOCKs; all others must be inherited' or reclassify the 2 entries as RULE/PLAN items.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.evidence_quote: > **절대 규칙**: sot 2/5-3_v12-Additions-Detail/ 내 파일은 **자체 LOCK을 정의하지 않는다**. 모든 LOCK-worthy 값은 해당 도메인 정본 폴더의 LOCK을 참조한다.

5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.defect: E/F/G item names in 상세명세 differ substantially from those in 종합계획서 and 06_v2-v3-advanced/_index.md. Examples: 상세명세 E-6 = 'Agent Collaboration Protocol' vs plan E-6 = 'Advanced Code Generation'; 상세명세 G-4 = 'Holographic Memory Model' vs plan G-4 = 'Bio-inspired Memory Architecture'. The LOCK-V12-10 mapping table tracks different items than the actual spec.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.why_objective: LOCK-V12-10 (master mapping table) and the index hub are tracking different items than the upstream technical specification, making Phase 2 귀속 확정 determinations target incorrect items.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.fix: Reconcile §6.5~§6.7, §A.2, and 06_v2-v3-advanced/_index.md against 상세명세 E/F/G item names, picking one authoritative source and updating all others.

5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.defect: AUTHORITY_CHAIN LOCK-V12-05 lists two different CBT distortion taxonomies internally: AUTHORITY_CHAIN §LOCK-V12-05 includes 'control fallacy, minimization/magnification' while 상세명세 distortion_types list includes 'blaming, fallacy_of_change, fallacy_of_fairness' with no overlap for several entries. The same LOCK value is inconsistent across the two authoritative documents.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.why_objective: Both documents claim to define the same LOCK-V12-05 value, but list different types. Any implementation reading one source will produce a different type set than one reading the other — this is an objective data inconsistency.
5-3_v12-Additions-Detail.fix: Establish one canonical 15-type list in the #9 Health-Wellness SOT domain, then synchronize AUTHORITY_CHAIN, 상세명세, and all index files to that single list.
